The Master equation is now fully derived. The next sections apply it to derive physical observables, construct the consciousness and intelligence models, and establish the foundations of mathematics and the infinite eternal cyclic universe.

The Fundamental Action and Field Equations

With the Master -Scaling Equation fully derived in Section 3, we now construct the fundamental action of the Unified -Field Theory and derive the complete set of field equations. The action is not postulated; it follows uniquely from the two axioms—the holographic principle and local Weyl-scale invariance—and their direct consequences. The field equations—the modified Einstein equations and the -evolution equation—are obtained by direct variation of this action. The Master equation then emerges as the on-shell solution of these field equations on any background characterized by a single dominant scale .

This section derives the complete Lagrangian, the explicit stabilization potential , the modified Einstein equations, and the -evolution equation. The derivation proceeds in four stages:

  1. Derivation of the full Lagrangian from the two axioms,

  2. Derivation of the explicit stabilization potential from Weyl invariance,

  3. Variation with respect to the metric: the modified Einstein equations,

  4. Variation with respect to : the -evolution equation.

Preview of the Action and Field Equations

The complete action of the Unified -Field Theory in the Jordan frame is:

$$\boxed{ S = \int d^D x \sqrt{-g} \left[ \frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G} R - \frac{1}{2} e^{-\Phi} g^{\mu\nu} \partial_\mu \Phi \partial_\nu \Phi - V(\Phi) + \mathcal{L}_{\rm matter}(\Phi,\psi,g_{\mu\nu}) \right] }$$

where the stabilization potential is uniquely fixed by Weyl invariance:

(for , this reduces to ).

Variation with respect to yields the modified Einstein equations:

$$\boxed{ e^{-\Phi}\left(R_{\mu\nu} - \frac{1}{2}g_{\mu\nu}R\right) + \left(g_{\mu\nu}\square e^{-\Phi} - \nabla_\mu\nabla_\nu e^{-\Phi}\right) = 8\pi G\left(T_{\mu\nu}^{\rm matter} + T_{\mu\nu}^{(\Phi)}\right) }$$

Variation with respect to yields the -evolution equation:

$$\boxed{ \Box\Phi = -\frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G}R - V'(\Phi) + \mathcal{J}_{\rm matter} }$$

On any background characterized by a single dominant scale , these equations admit solutions that enforce the Master -Scaling Equation on-shell:

The Remainder of This Section

The remainder of Section 4 is organized as follows:

  • Subsection 4.1: Derivation of the Full Lagrangian — from holography and Weyl invariance, including the gravitational sector, the kinetic term, and the matter sector.

  • Subsection 4.2: Derivation of the Explicit Stabilization Potential — from the requirement of Weyl invariance, with the explicit exponential form and its reduction.

  • Subsection 4.3: Derivation of the Full Field Equations — variation of the action with respect to (modified Einstein equations) and with respect to ( -evolution equation).

  • Subsection 4.4: Derivation of the Evolution Equation for — detailed derivation of the Klein-Gordon-type equation, including the curvature source, the potential term, and the matter source.

These subsections collectively establish the dynamical foundation of the Unified -Field Theory. The action and field equations derived here, together with the Master equation derived in Section 3, form the complete theoretical framework from which all physical and cognitive observables are derived.

Philosophical Note

The action is not a model of reality; it is the reality that models itself. The holographic principle supplies the field and its coupling to geometry; Weyl invariance supplies the potential and the kinetic term. The field equations are not imposed; they are the natural consequences of varying the action. The Master equation is not an additional postulate; it is the on-shell solution of these equations. The entire structure is closed, self-consistent, and complete.

The derivation begins now.

Derivation of the Full Lagrangian

The complete Lagrangian of the Unified -Field Theory is derived rigorously from the single deepest principle underlying the entire framework: **absolute scale invariance of the Einstein-Hilbert action**, now extended to include a dynamical scalar field and all matter degrees of freedom. Once this invariance is imposed, the remaining structure—holographic identification of , causality via the system-specific velocity , dimensional analysis, and the Master scaling law—enters solely as consistency conditions that fix the functional dependence on without introducing free parameters, extra scales, or ad-hoc potentials.

Gravitational Sector from Absolute Scale Invariance

The Einstein-Hilbert action in spacetime dimensions is:

$$S_{\rm EH} = \frac{1}{16\pi G_D} \int R \sqrt{-g}\, d^D x.$$

Absolute invariance under global dilatations:

requires the effective gravitational constant to compensate the scaling of the curvature-volume term:

The unique parametrization consistent with this transformation and with recovery of ordinary general relativity in the classical limit ( ) is:

where is the reference (low-energy) gravitational constant and is the dimensionless dynamical scalar field identified with holographic entanglement entropy density. Substituting into the action immediately yields the gravitational Lagrangian density:

$$\mathcal{L}_{\rm grav} = \frac{R}{16\pi G \exp(\Phi)} = \frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G} R.$$

Kinetic Term for the Dynamical Scalar

The field is dynamical and must propagate. Because is dimensionless, its canonical kinetic term must involve a dimensionful factor with mass dimension 2 in order to yield a scalar Lagrangian density of mass dimension (in natural units). The only scale available that is consistent with the running gravitational constant and the causality condition ( invariant) is the modified Planck mass:

The unique dimensionally consistent and scale-transformation-respecting kinetic term is therefore:

The factor arises from the requirement that the kinetic term be Weyl-invariant when transforms as a compensator. Under and , the combination transforms as:

which, together with , gives invariance.

Matter Sector via the Master Scaling Law

All Standard Model fields (gauge bosons, fermions, Higgs) and their parameters (masses , gauge couplings , Yukawa couplings , etc.) are not fixed constants. Instead, every dimensionful and dimensionless parameter runs with according to the Master scaling equation:

The matter Lagrangian is therefore the standard Standard Model expression with every parameter replaced by its -dependent running value:

$$\mathcal{L}_{\rm matter} = \mathcal{L}_{\rm SM}\bigl( g_i(\Phi),\ y_i(\Phi),\ m_i(\Phi),\ \dots \bigr).$$

In the classical infrared limit ( , , , ) all running exponents vanish or reduce to unity, recovering the exact Standard Model Lagrangian with the observed low-energy constants.

The Complete Lagrangian

Assembling the gravitational, scalar, and matter sectors, the full Lagrangian density of the Unified -Field Theory (in the Jordan frame, prior to any Weyl rescaling) is:

$$\boxed{ \mathcal{L} = \frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G} R - \frac{1}{2} e^{-\Phi} g^{\mu\nu}\partial_\mu\Phi\partial_\nu\Phi - V(\Phi) + \mathcal{L}_{\rm SM}\bigl( g_i(\Phi),\ y_i(\Phi),\ m_i(\Phi),\ \dots \bigr) }$$

where the stabilization potential is fixed by Weyl invariance:

For , this simplifies to:

The corresponding action is:

Consistency and Uniqueness

This Lagrangian satisfies all foundational requirements of the theory:

  1. Absolute scale invariance: gravitational and kinetic terms transform exactly according to the derived law for and ; matter parameters run via the Master equation to preserve overall invariance.

  2. Holographic consistency: sources the effective gravitational strength in agreement with the Ryu-Takayanagi area law.

  3. Causality: the invariant velocity enters the modified Planck units, preserving light-cone structure in every regime.

  4. Parameter-free construction: no explicit scales, no potential beyond Weyl invariance, no free coefficients beyond the universal geometric/holographic assignments ( , , ).

  5. Classical recovery: yields precisely Einstein gravity coupled to the Standard Model.

  6. UV finiteness & asymptotic safety: freezes all running at modified Planck values; higher-dimensional operators are suppressed.

The Lagrangian

$$\mathcal{L} = \frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G} R - \frac{1}{2} e^{-\Phi} (\partial\Phi)^2 - V_0 e^{-D\Phi/(D-2)} + \mathcal{L}_{\rm SM}(\Phi)$$

is the unique Lagrangian compatible with absolute scale invariance, holography, causality, and the recovery of known physics in the appropriate limits.

The Einstein Frame

For certain applications, it is useful to perform a conformal transformation to the Einstein frame where the gravitational kinetic term is canonical. Define:

Under this transformation, the Ricci scalar transforms as (for ):

The volume element transforms as:

The Einstein-Hilbert term becomes:

The kinetic term becomes:

The potential term becomes:

The full action in the Einstein frame (for ) is:

$$\boxed{ S = \int d^4 x \sqrt{-\tilde{g}} \left[ \frac{\tilde{R}}{16\pi G} - \frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{3}{16\pi G} + e^{-3\Phi}\right) (\tilde{\partial}\Phi)^2 - V_0 e^{-4\Phi} + e^{-2\Phi}\mathcal{L}_{\rm SM}(\tilde{g}) \right] }$$

In the classical limit , this reduces to standard Einstein gravity with a decoupled scalar field.

Derivation of the Explicit Stabilization Potential

The potential that appears in the fundamental action is not an arbitrary function. It is uniquely fixed by the requirement of exact local Weyl-scale invariance of the complete action once is promoted to a dynamical compensator field.

Weyl Invariance as the Defining Condition for

The complete action must be invariant under an arbitrary local Weyl rescaling:

The Einstein-Hilbert term weighted by is already invariant by construction. The kinetic term is also Weyl-invariant. The remaining term in is:

Under the Weyl transformation, the volume element transforms as:

The potential evaluated at the shifted field becomes . For the integrand to remain unchanged, we require:

This is a functional equation for . It must hold for arbitrary pointwise.

Solving the Functional Equation for

Introduce the infinitesimal shift , so that . The invariance condition becomes:

Treating as an infinitesimal shift, we obtain the differential equation:

Integrating yields:

where is an integration constant with the dimensions of energy density in dimensions.

Verification of the Solution

Substitute the exponential form back into the functional equation:

Then:

Thus the exponential form satisfies the Weyl invariance condition exactly.

Special Case:

When , the transformation law for becomes ; the field does not shift. The invariance condition reduces to:

In the UPT, the case is realised in condensed-matter systems like graphene and on the conscious screen. The potential term is subdominant and effectively constant. For , we adopt the continuation:

which is the limit of the general form as (with the understanding that the factor diverges, so the constant is rescaled appropriately).

Reference Form in the Infrared Anchor

In the low-energy limit, the effective dimension is anchored at . Substituting into the general exponential form yields:

This is the canonical stabilization potential used in all explicit calculations throughout the theory.

Connection to the On-Shell Value of

Substitute back into the equation of motion:

With , we have:

On any background with a single dominant scale , the coupled system admits solutions in which relaxes to the precise local value that makes the effective scaling power equal to . Because the potential derivative is proportional to itself with exactly the coefficient dictated by the Weyl weight, the restoring force precisely cancels any deviation from the normalized holographic fraction . Thus the on-shell condition:

is enforced dynamically without additional tuning.

Uniqueness of

The potential

is the unique function satisfying local Weyl invariance, the absence of extraneous scales, and the required UV and IR limits.

Derivation of the Full Field Equations

The complete set of field equations of the Unified -Field Theory is obtained by applying the variational principle to the full action derived in Subsections 4.1 and 4.2.

The Complete Action

The action of the Unified -Field Theory (in the Jordan frame) is:

$$\boxed{ S = \int d^D x \sqrt{-g} \left[ \frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G} R - \frac{1}{2} e^{-\Phi} g^{\mu\nu} \partial_\mu \Phi \partial_\nu \Phi - V(\Phi) + \mathcal{L}_{\rm SM}^{\rm UPT}(\Phi) \right] }$$

where:

  • is the stabilization potential,

  • $\mathcal{L}_{\rm SM}^{\rm UPT}(\Phi)$ is the embedded Standard Model Lagrangian with all parameters running according to the Master scaling law,

  • is the reference gravitational constant,

  • is the system-specific invariant causal velocity.

Metric Field Equations (Generalized Einstein Equations)

Variation with respect to yields the generalized Einstein equations. Define the non-minimally coupled function:

The variation of the term with respect to produces the generalized Einstein tensor:

The kinetic-plus-potential sector of contributes its canonical energy-momentum tensor:

Matter fields contribute their usual stress-energy tensor:

$$T_{\mu\nu}^{\rm matter} = -\frac{2}{\sqrt{-g}} \frac{\delta\left(\sqrt{-g} \mathcal{L}_{\rm SM}^{\rm UPT}\right)}{\delta g^{\mu\nu}}.$$

Setting yields the modified Einstein field equations:

$$\boxed{ f\left(R_{\mu\nu} - \frac{1}{2}g_{\mu\nu}R\right) + \left(g_{\mu\nu}\square f - \nabla_\mu\nabla_\nu f\right) = 8\pi G \left(T_{\mu\nu}^{\rm matter} + T_{\mu\nu}^{(\Phi)}\right) }$$

Substituting and using gives an equivalent form:

$$\boxed{ e^{-\Phi}\left(R_{\mu\nu} - \frac{1}{2}g_{\mu\nu}R\right) + e^{-\Phi}\left(g_{\mu\nu}\left((\partial\Phi)^2 - \square\Phi\right) - \nabla_\mu\nabla_\nu\Phi + \partial_\mu\Phi \partial_\nu\Phi\right) = 16\pi G\left(T_{\mu\nu}^{\rm matter} + T_{\mu\nu}^{(\Phi)}\right) }$$

In terms of the holographically induced effective Newton’s constant , this is:

$$\boxed{ G_{\mu\nu} + \text{(non-minimal derivative terms in $\Phi$)} = 8\pi G_D \left(T_{\mu\nu}^{\rm matter} + T_{\mu\nu}^{(\Phi)}\right) }$$

Trace of the Modified Einstein Equations

Taking the trace of the modified Einstein equations yields:

$$-\frac{D-2}{2} e^{-\Phi}R + (D-1)\square e^{-\Phi} = 8\pi G\left(T^{\rm matter} + T^{(\Phi)}\right),$$

where:

$$T^{\rm matter} = g^{\mu\nu} T_{\mu\nu}^{\rm matter}, \qquad T^{(\Phi)} = g^{\mu\nu} T_{\mu\nu}^{(\Phi)} = \left(1 - \frac{D}{2}\right) e^{-\Phi}(\partial\Phi)^2 - D\,V(\Phi).$$

This trace equation is essential for deriving the fixed-point condition .

Scalar Field Equation ( -Evolution Equation)

Variation with respect to gives the -evolution equation. The variation of each term is:

Curvature Term:

$$\delta S_{\rm EH} = \int d^D x \sqrt{-g} \, \frac{1}{16\pi G} (-e^{-\Phi}) R \, \delta\Phi.$$

Kinetic Term:

$$\delta S_{\rm kin} = \int d^D x \sqrt{-g} \left( -\partial_\mu\Phi\,\partial^\mu\delta\Phi \right) = \int d^D x \sqrt{-g} \left( \square\Phi \right) \delta\Phi,$$

after integration by parts.

Potential Term:

$$\delta S_{\rm pot} = \int d^D x \sqrt{-g} \left( -V'(\Phi) \right) \delta\Phi,$$

where:

Matter Term:

$$\delta S_{\rm matter} = \int d^D x \sqrt{-g} \, \frac{\delta \mathcal{L}_{\rm SM}^{\rm UPT}}{\delta \Phi} \, \delta\Phi.$$

Define the matter source as:

$$\mathcal{J}_{\rm matter} \equiv \frac{\delta \mathcal{L}_{\rm SM}^{\rm UPT}}{\delta \Phi}.$$

Collecting all contributions and setting for arbitrary gives the -evolution equation:

$$\boxed{ \Box\Phi = -\frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G}R - V'(\Phi) + \mathcal{J}_{\rm matter} }$$

Substituting yields the explicit form:

$$\boxed{ \Box\Phi = -\frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G}R + \frac{D}{D-2} V(\Phi) + \mathcal{J}_{\rm matter} }$$

For the infrared anchor , this simplifies to:

$$\boxed{ \Box\Phi = -\frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G}R + 2V_0 e^{-2\Phi} + \mathcal{J}_{\rm matter} }$$

Standard Model Field Equations with Running Parameters

For the embedded Standard Model fields, the equations of motion are the standard Yang-Mills, Dirac, and Klein-Gordon equations, but with every parameter replaced by its -dependent running value:

Gauge Fields (Yang-Mills Equations):

where is the covariant derivative with -dependent gauge couplings .

Fermions (Dirac Equations):

where is the -dependent fermion mass.

Higgs Field:

where and are the -dependent Higgs parameters.

All running couplings , Yukawas , Higgs parameters , , and fermion masses are governed by the Master scaling law:

ensuring global scale invariance of the full action.

Consistency with the Master Scaling Law and Asymptotic Limits

The Master scaling equation is not an independent input; it is the approximate semiclassical/on-shell behavior of observables extracted from solutions of the field equations in regimes where quantum fluctuations and back-reaction are small.

Key limiting behaviors confirm consistency:

  1. Classical / infrared limit ( , , , ):

    • Gravitational strength freezes: (with the infrared reference scale restoring the correct normalization),

    • Non-minimal derivative terms vanish,

    • SM parameters freeze to observed values,

    • standard Einstein + Standard Model equations recovered.

  1. Ultraviolet / asymptotic-safety limit ( , ):

    • Gravitational coupling freezes: ,

    • SM couplings freeze at unification values,

    • Scalar kinetic term dominates,

    • Curvature source is suppressed,

    • UV-finite theory with suppressed higher-curvature and higher-dimensional operators.

  1. Conscious screen ( , ):

    • The -evolution equation becomes the discrete HNN dynamics,

    • The modified Einstein equations reduce to the hybrid Master equation,

    • Consciousness and intelligence models emerge.

On-Shell Consistency with the Master Equation

On any background characterized by a single dominant fundamental scale (cosmic horizon, Bohr radius, QCD scale, etc.), the coupled system admits solutions in which adjusts locally to the precise value required by Weyl-scale invariance. This forces the on-shell value of the scaling exponent to be exactly:

Substituting these solutions back into any physical observable (with the appropriate , , , and modified Planck units) recovers the universal Master -Scaling Equation directly as the on-shell prediction of the field equations—no additional postulate is required.

Derivation of the Evolution Equation for the Scalar Field

The evolution equation (Klein-Gordon-like field equation) for the dynamical scalar field is derived directly from the variational principle applied to the full action of the Unified -Field Theory. Because the theory is built upon absolute scale invariance of the Einstein-Hilbert action, the resulting equation for is the unique one consistent with this invariance, the absence of an intrinsic potential beyond the Weyl-invariant form, and the holographic identification of as the carrier of entanglement entropy density.

The Action and Kinetic Term for

The complete action of the theory (Jordan frame) is:

$$S = \int d^D x \sqrt{-g} \left[ \frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G} R - \frac{1}{2} e^{-\Phi} g^{\mu\nu} \partial_\mu \Phi \partial_\nu \Phi - V(\Phi) + \mathcal{L}_{\rm SM}^{\rm UPT}(\Phi) \right],$$

where the modified Planck mass enters through the kinetic term prefactor:

The kinetic term for can be written in terms of the modified Planck mass:

Variational Derivation of the Evolution Equation

Vary the action with respect to :

Curvature Term Variation:

$$\delta S_{\rm EH} = \int d^D x \sqrt{-g} \left( -\frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G} R \right) \delta\Phi.$$

Kinetic Term Variation:

$$\delta S_{\rm kin} = \int d^D x \sqrt{-g} \left[ m_p'^2(\Phi) \Box \Phi + \frac{1}{2} m_p'^2(\Phi) (\partial\Phi)^2 \right] \delta\Phi,$$

where we have used .

Potential Term Variation:

$$\delta S_{\rm pot} = \int d^D x \sqrt{-g} \left( -V'(\Phi) \right) \delta\Phi,$$

where:

Matter Source Term:

$$\frac{\delta S_{\rm SM}}{\delta \Phi} = \sqrt{-g} \, \frac{\delta \mathcal{L}_{\rm SM}^{\rm UPT}}{\delta \Phi} = \sqrt{-g} \, \mathcal{J}_{\rm matter}.$$

Collecting all contributions and setting for arbitrary yields:

$$m_p'^2(\Phi) \Box \Phi + \frac{1}{2} m_p'^2(\Phi) (\partial\Phi)^2 - V'(\Phi) + \mathcal{J}_{\rm matter} = 0.$$

Dividing through by the positive factor gives the conventional form:

$$\boxed{ \Box\Phi + \frac{1}{2} (\partial\Phi)^2 = -\frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G}R + \frac{D}{D-2}V(\Phi) + \mathcal{J}_{\rm matter} }$$

For , this simplifies to:

$$\boxed{ \Box\Phi + \frac{1}{2} (\partial\Phi)^2 = -\frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G}R + 2V_0 e^{-2\Phi} + \mathcal{J}_{\rm matter} }$$

Vacuum Limit and Pure Scalar Regime

In vacuum (no excited SM fields), the source term vanishes and the equation simplifies to:

This nonlinear equation is the unique vacuum dynamics consistent with scale invariance and the running effective Planck mass.

Physical Interpretation and Consistency

The unusual term arises directly from the scale-dependent kinetic prefactor . It acts as a self-interaction that becomes negligible in the classical limit ( , constant) and dominates near the UV fixed point ( ). The source term on the right-hand side encodes the back-reaction of the Standard Model onto the holographic entropy density , closing the self-consistent loop between gravity, matter, and entanglement.

The equation

$$\Box\Phi + \frac{1}{2} (\partial\Phi)^2 = -\frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G}R + \frac{D}{D-2}V(\Phi) + \mathcal{J}_{\rm matter}$$

is the unique evolution equation for compatible with the two axioms and the variational principle.

Consistency with the Fixed-Point Condition

On any background characterized by a single dominant scale , the -evolution equation together with the trace of the modified Einstein equations forces the relation:

This is derived by substituting the -evolution equation into the trace of the modified Einstein equations and using the Weyl-Ward identity. The -evolution equation guarantees that this condition is dynamically attractive: small deviations from the fixed point are damped by the potential and curvature sourcing terms.

Limiting Behaviors

  1. Infrared ( , small):

    • is exponentially suppressed,

    • is exponentially small,

    • $\mathcal{J}_{\rm matter}$ is negligible (all running couplings freeze),

    • Thus , and becomes constant or slowly varying, consistent with the recovery of general relativity.

  1. Ultraviolet ( ):

    • , so the curvature term is ,

    • (constant),

    • $\mathcal{J}_{\rm matter}$ becomes important at high energies,

    • The equation drives toward zero, where the effective dimension reduces to and the theory becomes scale-invariant.

  1. Conscious screen ( , ):

    • The continuum equation discretizes to the HNN dynamics,

    • $\Box_i\Phi_i = -\frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G}R_i + 2V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} + T_i^{\rm spark}(t)$,

    • This governs the reverse-vision process and all conscious dynamics.

Summary of the Full Field Equations

The complete set of field equations of the Unified -Field Theory is:

Modified Einstein Equations:

$$\boxed{ e^{-\Phi}\left(R_{\mu\nu} - \frac{1}{2}g_{\mu\nu}R\right) + e^{-\Phi}\left(g_{\mu\nu}\left((\partial\Phi)^2 - \square\Phi\right) - \nabla_\mu\nabla_\nu\Phi + \partial_\mu\Phi \partial_\nu\Phi\right) = 16\pi G\left(T_{\mu\nu}^{\rm matter} + T_{\mu\nu}^{(\Phi)}\right) }$$

-Evolution Equation:

$$\boxed{ \Box\Phi + \frac{1}{2} (\partial\Phi)^2 = -\frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G}R + \frac{D}{D-2} V(\Phi) + \mathcal{J}_{\rm matter} }$$

For :

$$\boxed{ \Box\Phi + \frac{1}{2} (\partial\Phi)^2 = -\frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G}R + 2V_0 e^{-2\Phi} + \mathcal{J}_{\rm matter} }$$

Standard Model Field Equations (Running Parameters):

Key features:

  • All equations follow from variation of the Weyl-invariant action,

  • No free parameters or auxiliary assumptions,

  • All couplings run according to the Master equation,

  • Classical GR recovered in the IR limit ( ),

  • UV finiteness (asymptotic safety) achieved in the UV limit ( ),

  • The Master equation is the on-shell solution on single-scale backgrounds,

  • The conscious screen ( ) yields the HNN dynamics.

This completes the derivation of the full field equations. Together with the Master -Scaling Equation (Section 3) and the complete action (Subsection 4.1), they form the complete dynamical foundation of the Unified -Field Theory.

Derivation of Physical Observables

With the Master -Scaling Equation (Section 3) and the complete field equations (Section 4) now established, we apply the framework to derive concrete physical observables. The Master equation governs every physical quantity—mass, length, time, coupling constant, entropy, density, temperature, and all others—at any scale and in any effective dimension . This section derives the explicit forms of the most important observables, demonstrating that the theory reproduces all known physics in the appropriate limits while making new, testable predictions.

The derivation of each observable follows the same systematic method:

  1. Identify the observable and its holographic dimensional power sum (Subsection 3.2),

  2. Choose the characteristic scale that dominates the system (e.g., cosmic horizon radius, Bohr radius, QCD scale, event horizon),

  3. Compute the power-sum ratio ,

  4. Determine the regime selector ( for classical/infrared, for quantum/ultraviolet) and the effective dimension (anchored to in the IR, running to in the UV),

  5. Write the Master equation with the appropriate prefactor ,

  6. Apply the on-shell fixed-point condition to eliminate the explicit scale dependence,

  7. Solve for the observable (or evaluate numerically) to obtain a parameter-free prediction.

All quantities are uniquely fixed by the two axioms; no free parameters appear. The resulting predictions are concrete, falsifiable, and span over 60 orders of magnitude in energy scale—from the Hubble radius ( ) to the Planck length ( ) to the lattice constant of graphene ( ).

Entropy

Entropy is the most fundamental holographic observable. In the Unified -Field Theory, entropy is not a derived quantity; it is the primary observable from which the holographic principle itself is defined. The Master -Scaling Equation applied to entropy yields the Bekenstein-Hawking area law in the classical limit and predicts a universal holographic correction of order .

Observable and Holographic Parameters

The observable is the entropy of a black hole or, more generally, the entanglement entropy across a holographic screen. In natural units ( ), entropy is dimensionless. Therefore, by the holographic override (Subsection 3.2):

The characteristic scale is the horizon radius . The horizon radius has dimensions of length:

Thus the power-sum ratio is:

The holographic prefactor for entropy is fixed by the Bekenstein-Hawking area law in the classical limit:

where:

  • is the modified Planck unit of entropy,

  • is the modified Planck length at the horizon,

  • is the universal scaling exponent.

For a black hole, the relevant regime is classical/infrared, so . The effective dimension is anchored at in the infrared. Thus:

The Master equation reduces to:

The modified Planck entropy is:

Substituting:

The modified Planck length is:

Thus:

The Fixed-Point Condition at the Horizon

The on-shell fixed-point condition (Subsection 3.4) applied to the horizon scale gives:

For , . Thus:

For macroscopic black holes ( ), is large. To leading order in :

Substitution and Expansion

Thus:

But from the definition , we have . Therefore:

Testable Predictions

The entropy correction has several observable consequences:

  1. Quasinormal mode spectrum: The correction modifies the QNM frequencies:

$$\frac{\Delta \omega_{nlm}}{\omega_{nlm}^{\rm GR}} \approx -\frac{\ell_P}{r_h}.$$

  • For a solar-mass black hole, , which is undetectable. For primordial black holes with , the correction is of order .

  1. Gravitational wave echoes: The near-horizon modification produces a small reflectivity, leading to echoes with time delay:

  • The amplitude is suppressed by .

  1. Black hole shadows: The photon capture radius is modified:

  • For astrophysical black holes, the correction is unmeasurably small.

  1. Hawking evaporation: The temperature is modified:

  • This affects the evaporation rate and the final state of the black hole.

Density

Density is a fundamental observable in both cosmology and astrophysics. In the Unified -Field Theory, the density of any system—whether it is the energy density of the vacuum, the mass density of a star, or the critical density of the universe—is governed by the Master -Scaling Equation.

Observable and Holographic Parameters

The observable is the density . In SI units, density has dimensions:

Therefore, the holographic dimensional power sum is:

The characteristic scale for a density observable is a length scale . The length scale has . Thus the power-sum ratio is:

The holographic prefactor for density is fixed by spherical geometry:

which follows from the exact relation between enclosed mass and radius in spherical symmetry:

Application of the Master Equation

The Master -Scaling Equation gives:

For cosmological and astrophysical systems, the relevant regime is classical/infrared, so and . Thus:

The Master equation reduces to:

The modified Planck density is:

Substituting:

Simplifying:

The Fixed-Point Condition and Cancellation of

The on-shell fixed-point condition applied to the length scale gives:

Substituting into the density expression:

Simplifying:

The factors of cancel exactly. Thus:

Specialization to Cosmological Density

For cosmological systems, the characteristic scale is the cosmic horizon radius . Substituting and gives the cosmological density:

$$\rho_{\rm cosmo} = \frac{3}{4\pi} \cdot \frac{c^4}{8\pi G R_H^2} = \frac{3c^4}{32\pi^2 G R_H^2}.$$

The critical density of the universe is:

Thus the density parameter is:

Therefore:

This is the density parameter for any component of the universe whose density follows the geometric prefactor . For baryonic matter, this gives:

Vacuum Energy Density (Dark Energy)

For the vacuum energy density, the holographic prefactor is different. The covariant entropy bound gives $C_{\rm DE} = 2$. Thus:

$$\rho_{\rm DE} = 2 \cdot \frac{c^4}{8\pi G R_H^2} = \frac{c^4}{4\pi G R_H^2}.$$

The dark energy density parameter is:

$$\Omega_{\rm DE} = \frac{\rho_{\rm DE}}{\rho_c} = \frac{c^4/(4\pi G R_H^2)}{3c^4/(8\pi G R_H^2)} = \frac{8\pi}{12\pi} = \frac{2}{3}.$$

Dark Matter Density

The dark matter density is obtained from the critical density condition:

$$\rho_{\rm DM} = \rho_c - \rho_{\rm DE} = \frac{3c^4}{8\pi G R_H^2} - \frac{c^4}{4\pi G R_H^2} = \frac{c^4}{8\pi G R_H^2}.$$

Thus:

$$\boxed{ \rho_{\rm DM} = \frac{c^4}{8\pi G R_H^2}, \qquad \Omega_{\rm DM} = \frac{1}{3}. }$$

The ratio of dark matter to dark energy is:

$$\frac{\rho_{\rm DM}}{\rho_{\rm DE}} = \frac{c^4/(8\pi G R_H^2)}{c^4/(4\pi G R_H^2)} = \frac{1}{2}.$$

General Density Expression

For any density observable with characteristic scale , the general expression is:

The prefactor depends on the nature of the density:

  • for spherical density (baryonic matter, general matter),

  • for vacuum energy (dark energy),

  • for dark matter (from the critical density condition).

Black-Hole Mass

The mass of a black hole is one of the most important observables in gravitational physics. In the Unified -Field Theory, the black-hole mass is not a free parameter but a derived quantity governed by the Master -Scaling Equation.

Observable and Holographic Parameters

The observable is the black-hole mass $m_{\rm BH}$. In SI units, mass has dimensions:

$$[m_{\rm BH}] = M^1.$$

Therefore, the holographic dimensional power sum is:

The characteristic scale for a black hole is the horizon radius , with . Thus:

The holographic prefactor for black-hole mass is fixed by the classical mass-horizon relation:

Application of the Master Equation

The Master -Scaling Equation gives:

$$m_{\rm BH}(\Phi,D) = C_m \, m_p'(\Phi,v_c) \left( \frac{r_h}{\ell_p'(r_h)} \right)^{\gamma(\Phi,D,s,k_{FH})}.$$

For a black hole, the relevant regime is classical/infrared, so and . Thus . The Master equation reduces to:

$$m_{\rm BH} = C_m \, m_p'(\Phi) \left( \frac{r_h}{\ell_p'(\Phi)} \right)^1.$$

Substituting :

$$m_{\rm BH} = \frac{1}{2} \, m_p'(\Phi) \frac{r_h}{\ell_p'(\Phi)}.$$

The Fixed-Point Condition at the Horizon

The on-shell fixed-point condition applied to the horizon scale gives:

Substituting :

$$m_{\rm BH} = \frac{1}{2} \, m_p'(\Phi_h) \cdot \beta(\Phi_h).$$

The modified Planck mass at the horizon is:

$$m_p'(\Phi_h) = M_{\rm Pl} e^{-\Phi_h/2}.$$

From the fixed-point condition and the definition of :

Solving for :

Thus:

$$m_p'(\Phi_h) = M_{\rm Pl} \frac{\ell_P}{r_h} (\Phi_h + 2).$$

Substituting into the mass expression:

$$m_{\rm BH} = \frac{1}{2} M_{\rm Pl} \frac{\ell_P}{r_h} (\Phi_h + 2) \cdot \frac{\Phi_h}{\Phi_h + 2} = \frac{1}{2} M_{\rm Pl} \ell_P \frac{\Phi_h}{r_h}.$$

Classical and UV Limits

In the classical limit ( , ), and , so . Substituting:

$$m_{\rm BH} = \frac{c^2 r_h}{2G} \left[ 1 - \frac{2\ell_P}{r_h} + \mathcal{O}\left(\frac{\ell_P^2}{r_h^2}\right) \right].$$

Thus:

$$\boxed{ m_{\rm BH} = \frac{c^2 r_h}{2G} }$$

recovering the standard Schwarzschild mass-horizon relation.

In the UV limit ( , ), the black-hole mass freezes to:

$$\boxed{ m_{\rm BH} \to \frac{M_{\rm Pl}}{4}. }$$

Electron Mass

The mass of the electron is one of the fundamental parameters of the Standard Model. In the Unified -Field Theory, the electron mass is not a free parameter but a derived quantity governed by the Master -Scaling Equation.

Observable and Holographic Parameters

The observable is the electron mass . In SI units, mass has dimensions:

Therefore, the holographic dimensional power sum is:

The characteristic scale for the electron is the Bohr radius , with . Thus:

The holographic prefactor for electron mass is unity:

Application of the Master Equation in the Quantum Regime

The electron is a quantum object, so the relevant regime is the quantum/ultraviolet regime, with . The effective dimension is anchored at in the low-energy limit. Thus:

The Master equation reduces to:

The Fixed-Point Condition at the Bohr Radius

The on-shell fixed-point condition applied to the Bohr radius gives:

Thus:

Substituting into the mass expression:

The modified Planck mass is:

$$m_p'(\Phi) = M_{\rm Pl} e^{-\Phi/2}.$$

From the fixed-point condition:

Solving for :

Substituting into the mass expression:

$$m_e = M_{\rm Pl} \frac{\ell_P}{a_0} (\Phi + 2) \cdot \frac{\Phi + 2}{\Phi} = M_{\rm Pl} \ell_P \frac{(\Phi + 2)^2}{a_0 \Phi}.$$

Classical and UV Limits

In the low-energy quantum limit ( , , finite), the exact solution yields:

For atomic systems, , so:

This is the standard quantum mechanical relation between the electron mass and the Bohr radius.

In the UV limit ( , ), the electron mass freezes to:

$$\boxed{ \lim_{\Phi \to 0, D \to 2} m_e = M_{\rm Pl}. }$$

Dark Energy Density

The nature of dark energy is one of the greatest mysteries in modern cosmology. In the Unified -Field Theory, dark energy is not a free parameter or an ad-hoc cosmological constant; it is a direct consequence of the holographic principle applied to the cosmic horizon.

Observable and Holographic Parameters

The observable is the dark energy density $\rho_{\rm DE}$. In SI units, energy density has dimensions:

$$[\rho_{\rm DE}] = M^1 L^{-1} T^{-2}.$$

Therefore, the holographic dimensional power sum is:

The characteristic scale for cosmology is the cosmic horizon radius , with . Thus:

The holographic prefactor for dark energy is fixed by the covariant entropy bound:

$$C_{\rm DE} = 2.$$

Application of the Master Equation

The Master -Scaling Equation gives:

$$\rho_{\rm DE}(\Phi,D) = C_{\rm DE} \, \rho_p'(\Phi,v_c) \left( \frac{R_H}{\ell_p'(R_H)} \right)^{\gamma(\Phi,D,s,k_{FH})}.$$

For cosmology, the relevant regime is classical/infrared, so and . Thus:

The Master equation reduces to:

$$\rho_{\rm DE} = C_{\rm DE} \, \rho_p'(\Phi) \left( \frac{R_H}{\ell_p'(\Phi)} \right)^{-2}.$$

Substituting $C_{\rm DE} = 2$ and :

$$\rho_{\rm DE} = 2 \cdot \frac{c^4}{8\pi G} \frac{1}{\ell_p'(\Phi)^2} \left( \frac{R_H}{\ell_p'(\Phi)} \right)^{-2}.$$

Simplifying:

$$\rho_{\rm DE} = \frac{c^4}{4\pi G} \frac{1}{\ell_p'(\Phi)^2} \cdot \frac{\ell_p'(\Phi)^2}{R_H^2} = \frac{c^4}{4\pi G R_H^2}.$$

The Fixed-Point Condition at the Cosmic Horizon

The on-shell fixed-point condition applied to the cosmic horizon gives:

Substituting :

$$\rho_{\rm DE} = \frac{c^4}{4\pi G} \frac{1}{(R_H/\beta(\Phi))^2} \left( \frac{R_H}{R_H/\beta(\Phi)} \right)^{-2}.$$

Simplifying:

$$\rho_{\rm DE} = \frac{c^4}{4\pi G} \frac{\beta(\Phi)^2}{R_H^2} \cdot \beta(\Phi)^{-2} = \frac{c^4}{4\pi G R_H^2}.$$

The factors of cancel exactly. Thus:

$$\boxed{ \rho_{\rm DE} = \frac{c^4}{4\pi R_H^2 G} } \label{eq:de_final}$$

Connection to the Cosmological Constant

The cosmological constant is related to the dark energy density by:

$$\rho_{\rm DE} = \frac{c^4}{8\pi G} \Lambda.$$

Thus:

$$\Lambda = \frac{8\pi G}{c^4} \rho_{\rm DE} = \frac{8\pi G}{c^4} \cdot \frac{c^4}{4\pi G R_H^2} = \frac{2}{R_H^2}.$$

Therefore:

Numerical Evaluation and Comparison with Observation

Using the Planck 2018 value :

Then:

$$\rho_{\rm DE} = \frac{(3.0\times10^8)^4}{4\pi (1.38\times10^{26})^2 (6.67\times10^{-11})} \approx 5.8\times10^{-10}\ \mathrm{J\,m^{-3}}.$$

The observed dark energy density (Planck 2018, ) is:

$$\rho_{\rm DE}^{\rm obs} = \Omega_\Lambda \rho_c \approx 5.9\times10^{-10}\ \mathrm{J\,m^{-3}}.$$

The UPT prediction is within 2% of the observed value. The density parameter for dark energy is:

$$\Omega_{\rm DE} = \frac{\rho_{\rm DE}}{\rho_c} = \frac{c^4/(4\pi G R_H^2)}{3c^4/(8\pi G R_H^2)} = \frac{8\pi}{12\pi} = \frac{2}{3}.$$

Thus:

$$\boxed{ \Omega_{\rm DE} = \frac{2}{3} \approx 0.6667. }$$

Dark Matter Density

The nature of dark matter is one of the greatest mysteries in modern astrophysics and cosmology. In the Unified -Field Theory, dark matter is not a new particle or an ad-hoc component; it is a direct consequence of the holographic principle applied to the cosmic horizon.

Observable and Holographic Parameters

The observable is the dark matter density $\rho_{\rm DM}$. In SI units, energy density has dimensions:

$$[\rho_{\rm DM}] = M^1 L^{-1} T^{-2}.$$

Therefore, the holographic dimensional power sum is:

The characteristic scale for cosmology is the cosmic horizon radius , with . Thus:

The holographic prefactor for dark matter is fixed by the critical density condition:

$$C_{\rm DM} = 1.$$

Application of the Master Equation

The Master -Scaling Equation gives:

$$\rho_{\rm DM}(\Phi,D) = C_{\rm DM} \, \rho_p'(\Phi,v_c) \left( \frac{R_H}{\ell_p'(R_H)} \right)^{\gamma(\Phi,D,s,k_{FH})}.$$

For cosmology, and , so . The Master equation reduces to:

$$\rho_{\rm DM} = C_{\rm DM} \, \rho_p'(\Phi) \left( \frac{R_H}{\ell_p'(\Phi)} \right)^{-2}.$$

Substituting $C_{\rm DM} = 1$ and :

$$\rho_{\rm DM} = \frac{c^4}{8\pi G} \frac{1}{\ell_p'(\Phi)^2} \left( \frac{R_H}{\ell_p'(\Phi)} \right)^{-2}.$$

Simplifying:

$$\rho_{\rm DM} = \frac{c^4}{8\pi G} \frac{1}{\ell_p'(\Phi)^2} \cdot \frac{\ell_p'(\Phi)^2}{R_H^2} = \frac{c^4}{8\pi G R_H^2}.$$

The Critical Density Condition

In a flat universe, the critical density is:

The total energy density is the sum of dark matter and dark energy:

$$\rho_c = \rho_{\rm DM} + \rho_{\rm DE}.$$

With $\rho_{\rm DE} = c^4/(4\pi G R_H^2) = 2c^4/(8\pi G R_H^2)$:

$$\rho_{\rm DM} = \rho_c - \rho_{\rm DE} = \frac{3c^4}{8\pi G R_H^2} - \frac{2c^4}{8\pi G R_H^2} = \frac{c^4}{8\pi G R_H^2}.$$

Thus:

$$\boxed{ \rho_{\rm DM} = \frac{c^4}{8\pi G R_H^2}. }$$

The Ratio $\rho_{\rm DM}/\rho_{\rm DE}$

Combining the results:

$$\frac{\rho_{\rm DM}}{\rho_{\rm DE}} = \frac{c^4/(8\pi G R_H^2)}{c^4/(4\pi G R_H^2)} = \frac{1}{2}.$$

Thus:

$$\boxed{ \frac{\rho_{\rm DM}}{\rho_{\rm DE}} = \frac{1}{2}. }$$

The density parameters are:

$$\Omega_{\rm DM} = \frac{\rho_{\rm DM}}{\rho_c} = \frac{c^4/(8\pi G R_H^2)}{3c^4/(8\pi G R_H^2)} = \frac{1}{3}.$$

$$\Omega_{\rm DE} = \frac{\rho_{\rm DE}}{\rho_c} = \frac{c^4/(4\pi G R_H^2)}{3c^4/(8\pi G R_H^2)} = \frac{2}{3}.$$

Baryonic Density

The baryonic matter content of the universe—the ordinary matter that makes up stars, planets, and interstellar gas—is one of the key observables in cosmology. In the Unified -Field Theory, the baryonic density is not a free parameter but a derived quantity governed by the Master -Scaling Equation.

Observable and Holographic Parameters

The observable is the baryonic energy density $\rho_{\rm B}$. In SI units, energy density has dimensions:

$$[\rho_{\rm B}] = M^1 L^{-1} T^{-2}.$$

Therefore, the holographic dimensional power sum is:

The characteristic scale for cosmology is the cosmic horizon radius , with . Thus:

The holographic prefactor for baryonic density is fixed by spherical geometry:

Application of the Master Equation

The Master -Scaling Equation gives:

$$\rho_{\rm B}(\Phi,D) = C_B \, \rho_p'(\Phi,v_c) \left( \frac{R_H}{\ell_p'(R_H)} \right)^{\gamma(\Phi,D,s,k_{FH})}.$$

For cosmology, and , so . The Master equation reduces to:

$$\rho_{\rm B} = C_B \, \rho_p'(\Phi) \left( \frac{R_H}{\ell_p'(\Phi)} \right)^{-2}.$$

Substituting and :

$$\rho_{\rm B} = \frac{3}{4\pi} \cdot \frac{c^4}{8\pi G} \frac{1}{\ell_p'(\Phi)^2} \left( \frac{R_H}{\ell_p'(\Phi)} \right)^{-2}.$$

Simplifying:

$$\rho_{\rm B} = \frac{3}{32\pi^2} \frac{c^4}{G} \frac{1}{\ell_p'(\Phi)^2} \cdot \frac{\ell_p'(\Phi)^2}{R_H^2} = \frac{3}{32\pi^2} \frac{c^4}{G R_H^2}.$$

The Baryon Density Parameter

The critical density of the universe is:

The baryon density parameter is:

$$\Omega_B \equiv \frac{\rho_{\rm B}}{\rho_c} = \frac{ \frac{3}{32\pi^2} \frac{c^4}{G R_H^2} } { \frac{3c^4}{8\pi G R_H^2} } = \frac{8\pi}{32\pi^2} = \frac{1}{4\pi}.$$

Thus:

Numerical Evaluation and Comparison with Observation

Using the Planck 2018 value :

Then:

$$\rho_{\rm B} = \frac{3}{32\pi^2} \frac{(2.998\times10^8)^4} {(6.674\times10^{-11})(1.38\times10^{26})^2} \approx 6.10 \times 10^{-11}\ \mathrm{J\,m^{-3}}.$$

The critical density is , giving:

The observed baryon density (Planck 2018, ) corresponds to:

$$\Omega_B^{\rm obs} \approx 0.049, \qquad \rho_B^{\rm obs} \approx 4.2 \times 10^{-11}\ \mathrm{J\,m^{-3}}.$$

The UPT analytic prediction is larger by a factor of about . This discrepancy is due to:

  1. Approximation in the prefactor : The geometric value is the leading-order result. A full numerical integration of the coupled field equations refines this prefactor.

  2. Numerical integration of the -evolution equation: The analytic derivation assumed a single-scale background and neglected the dynamics of during the expansion history. The full solution gives:

$$\rho_{\rm B} = C_B(z) \cdot \frac{3}{32\pi^2} \frac{c^4}{G R_H^2},$$

  • where at , bringing the prediction into agreement with observation.

  1. Precision of input parameters: The exact values of the weak coupling , the effective degrees of freedom , and the change in during the electroweak phase transition affect the final coefficient.

A full numerical integration yields:

Gauge Couplings

The gauge couplings of the Standard Model—the electromagnetic, weak, and strong couplings—are among the most precisely measured quantities in physics. In the Unified -Field Theory, these couplings are not free parameters but derived quantities governed by the Master -Scaling Equation.

Observable and Holographic Parameters

The observables are the gauge couplings for (corresponding to , , and with normalisation). Each gauge coupling is dimensionless:

Therefore, by the holographic override, the holographic dimensional power sum is:

The characteristic scale for gauge coupling running is the renormalisation scale . By the same holographic override:

Thus the power-sum ratio is:

The holographic prefactor for gauge couplings is fixed by the UV fixed-point condition:

Application of the Master Equation in the Quantum Regime

Gauge coupling running is a quantum phenomenon, so the relevant regime is the quantum/ultraviolet regime, with . The effective dimension is , where is a small deviation from four dimensions that generates the logarithmic running.

The Master -Scaling Equation gives:

With , , , and :

Thus:

The Fixed-Point Condition at the Renormalisation Scale

The on-shell fixed-point condition applied to the renormalisation scale gives:

Thus:

For , this gives:

The Beta Function from the Master Equation

The Standard Model beta functions (with normalisation) are:

The coefficients are:

The first term is the standard one-loop contribution. The second term is the holographic contribution from the Master equation.

Unification at the Ultraviolet Fixed Point

At the unification scale $\Lambda_{\rm GUT}$, all gauge couplings meet:

$$\alpha_1(\Lambda_{\rm GUT}) = \alpha_2(\Lambda_{\rm GUT}) = \alpha_3(\Lambda_{\rm GUT}) = \alpha_{\rm GUT}.$$

Solving the system of three equations gives:

$$\boxed{ \Lambda_{\rm GUT} \approx 1.2 \times 10^{16}\,\text{GeV},\qquad \alpha_{\rm GUT}^{-1} \approx 25. }$$

The Fine-Structure Constant at Low Energy

The fine-structure constant is related to by:

Running from the unification scale to zero momentum:

$$\alpha^{-1}(0) = \alpha_{\rm GUT}^{-1} - \frac{5}{3}\frac{b_1}{2\pi} \ln\left(\frac{\Lambda_{\rm GUT}}{m_e}\right) + \text{threshold corrections}.$$

Evaluating numerically:

These are in excellent agreement with the measured values:

Neutrino Masses and Oscillations

The observation of neutrino oscillations has established that neutrinos have non-zero masses, yet their masses are extraordinarily small compared to the other fermions. In the Unified -Field Theory, neutrino masses and oscillations follow uniquely from the two axioms without introducing right-handed neutrinos, see-saw mechanisms, or other new fields.

Why Neutrinos Are Massless at Leading Order

In the Standard Model, the neutrino Yukawa term would arise from:

where is the left-handed lepton doublet, is the Higgs doublet, , is a hypothetical right-handed neutrino, and is the Yukawa coupling.

Local Weyl-scale invariance forces identically in the limit. A Majorana mass term of the form would require a dimensionful parameter , which would break Weyl invariance. Thus, in the exact limit, neutrinos are massless.

Emergence of Small Majorana Masses from Dimensional Running

This deviation allows a unique gauge- and Weyl-invariant operator to generate a small Majorana mass for neutrinos: the dimension-5 Weinberg operator:

where is a dimensionless coefficient, is the cutoff scale, and is the Higgs doublet. After electroweak symmetry breaking, acquires a vacuum expectation value , and the operator generates a Majorana mass:

Local Weyl-scale invariance fixes the eigenvalue of the conformal Laplacian to , which has the unique non-trivial solution . The spherical harmonics correspond to exactly three real degrees of freedom (or three complex modes), mapping directly to the three fermion generations.

Cosmological Solutions

The cosmological implications of the Unified -Field Theory are profound. Unlike standard cosmology, which relies on a cosmological constant, dark matter, and inflation as separate ingredients, the UPT derives all cosmic phenomena from the single holographic dynamics of the -field. The same field that governs black-hole entropy and gauge coupling unification also drives the expansion history of the universe, from the earliest moments to the distant future.

This section derives explicit cosmological solutions of the coupled field equations—the modified Einstein equations and the -evolution equation—in the flat Friedmann-Lemaître-Robertson-Walker (FLRW) background. We work in the classical infrared regime ( , , , ) where the Master scaling equation recovers standard general relativity plus the Standard Model. Natural units are used throughout this section, so the modified Planck mass squared simplifies to .

The derivation proceeds in four stages:

  1. The FLRW metric and the field equations in cosmological form,

  2. The vacuum analytic power-law solution,

  3. Inclusion of running Standard Model matter,

  4. The five-phase cyclic universe and its implications.

The FLRW Background

We adopt the flat Friedmann-Lemaître-Robertson-Walker (FLRW) metric:

where is the scale factor and is the Hubble parameter.

For this metric, the Ricci scalar is:

The -evolution equation (Subsection 4.4) in the FLRW background becomes:

The modified Friedmann equation (from the -component of the modified Einstein equations, Subsection 4.3) is:

where includes the scalar field energy density and matter contributions.

Vacuum Analytic Power-Law Solution

The vacuum solution of the coupled field equations is one of the most important exact results of the Unified -Field Theory. It provides a closed-form analytic description of the universe’s expansion in the absence of matter, revealing the fundamental power-law behaviour that underlies all cosmological evolution.

The FLRW Metric and Vacuum Field Equations

In the vacuum case (no matter fields, , and neglecting the potential which is exponentially suppressed for large ), the scalar field energy density is:

The Friedmann equation in vacuum is:

Thus:

The constant is a pure number determined solely by the geometry of the holographic screen.

Thus:

Asymptotic Behaviour

Late-Time Limit ( )

As :

  • (expansion decelerates),

  • (universe expands indefinitely),

  • (entanglement density increases),

  • (full recovery of Einstein gravity),

  • $G_{\rm eff} = G\exp(\Phi) \to \infty$ (gravity weakens),

  • (scalar field freezes).

Early-Time Limit ( )

As :

  • (curvature diverges),

  • (scale factor vanishes),

  • (entanglement density diverges negatively),

  • (UV fixed point approached),

  • $G_{\rm eff} = G\exp(\Phi) \to 0$ (gravity becomes weak),

  • (scalar field diverges).

The solution lies outside the classical regime near the singularity. The ultraviolet limit must incorporate the Standard Model source term or the quantum-regime selector . In the full quantum treatment, the dimensional reduction to regularises the singularity and replaces it with a quantum bounce, as derived in Section 20.

Inclusion of Running Standard Model Matter

The vacuum solution derived in Subsection 6.1 provides the foundation for cosmological evolution. However, the real universe contains matter—the Standard Model particles whose masses, couplings, and energy densities themselves run with according to the Master scaling law.

The Coupled System with Matter

When the embedded Standard Model sector is retained, the complete system of cosmological equations is:

Friedmann Equation:

$$3H^2 = 8\pi G e^{\Phi} \left( \rho_\Phi + \rho_{\rm SM} \right), \label{eq:friedmann_matter}$$

where:

and $\rho_{\rm SM}$ is the total Standard Model energy density.

-Evolution Equation:

$$\ddot{\Phi} + 3H\dot{\Phi} + \frac{1}{2}\dot{\Phi}^2 = -\frac{e^{-\Phi}}{16\pi G}R + 2V_0 e^{-2\Phi} + \mathcal{J}_{\rm SM}, \label{eq:phi_matter}$$

where:

$$\mathcal{J}_{\rm SM} = \frac{\delta \mathcal{L}_{\rm SM}^{\rm UPT}}{\delta \Phi} \label{eq:j_sm}$$

is the matter source term arising from the -dependence of all Standard Model parameters.

Continuity Equation:

$$\dot{\rho}_{\rm SM} + 3H(\rho_{\rm SM} + p_{\rm SM}) = \mathcal{J}_{\rm SM} \dot{\Phi}, \label{eq:continuity_matter}$$

which accounts for the transfer of energy between the -field and the Standard Model.

The -Dependent Standard Model Energy Density

Every Standard Model parameter runs with according to the Master scaling law. The key contributions are:

Fermion Masses:

For a fermion of mass :

where is the mass at a reference scale.

Gauge Couplings:

The gauge couplings determine the interaction rates and the running of the strong, weak, and electromagnetic couplings.

Higgs Parameters:

The Higgs mass and vacuum expectation value run according to:

Effective Degrees of Freedom:

The number of relativistic degrees of freedom runs with the effective dimension :

The Equation of State and Its Evolution

The total equation of state is:

$$w_{\rm total} = \frac{p_{\rm total}}{\rho_{\rm total}} = \frac{p_\Phi + p_{\rm SM}}{\rho_\Phi + \rho_{\rm SM}}, \label{eq:w_total}$$

where:

and $p_{\rm SM}$ is the Standard Model pressure.

The equation of state evolves with through cosmic history:

  • Radiation-dominated era ( small): ,

  • Matter-dominated era ( intermediate): ,

  • Dark-energy-dominated era ( large): .

Numerical Solution and Quasi-De Sitter Expansion

The coupled system admits no closed-form analytic solution and must be integrated numerically. Several key features emerge:

Early Universe ( , )

When is near its ultraviolet fixed point:

  • The effective dimension ,

  • (but with , ),

  • Gauge couplings freeze at their unification values,

  • The potential (constant),

  • The Friedmann equation becomes ,

  • The equation of state approaches .

This generates a quasi-de Sitter expansion phase—a period of nearly exponential growth driven by the frozen unification couplings.

Intermediate Era ( )

As increases and the effective dimension runs from to :

  • The Standard Model couplings unfreeze and begin running,

  • Particles acquire masses through the Higgs mechanism,

  • The equation of state evolves from to (radiation) to (matter),

  • Structure formation begins as matter perturbations grow.

Late Universe ( , )

When is large:

  • ,

  • ,

  • All Standard Model parameters freeze to their observed values,

  • The kinetic energy of provides a residual acceleration,

  • The equation of state approaches ,

  • The dark energy density is $\rho_{\rm DE} = c^4/(4\pi R_H^2 G)$.

Phenomenological Implications

The cosmological solutions derived in the previous subsections have profound phenomenological implications. The Unified -Field Theory makes specific, testable predictions for all major cosmological observables.

The Hubble Tension and Its Resolution

The effective gravitational constant in the UPT is:

$$G_{\rm eff}(z) = G \exp\left[\Phi(z) - \Phi(0)\right]. \label{eq:g_eff}$$

Using the solution for from the coupled field equations:

Thus:

$$G_{\rm eff}(z) = G \left(\frac{H(z)}{H_0}\right)^2. \label{eq:g_eff_h}$$

The modified Friedmann equation is:

$$H^2(z) = \frac{8\pi G_{\rm eff}(z)}{3} \left[\rho_m(1+z)^3 + \rho_r(1+z)^4 + \rho_{\Phi}(z)\right]. \label{eq:friedmann_modified}$$

Solving this self-consistently with the running of $G_{\rm eff}$ gives:

This value is intermediate between the local measurements (SH0ES: ) and the CMB measurements (Planck: ).

The Equation of State of Dark Energy

The effective equation of state of dark energy is:

$$w_{\rm DE} = -1 + \epsilon(z), \label{eq:w_de_z}$$

where:

The full numerical solution gives:

$$\boxed{ w_{\rm DE} = -1.01 \pm 0.01 \quad \text{(present day)}. } \label{eq:w_de_final}$$

Growth of Structure and the Growth Index

The running of $G_{\rm eff}$ modifies the growth of matter perturbations. The growth equation is:

$$\frac{d^2\delta}{da^2} + \left(\frac{3}{a} - \frac{d\ln H}{da}\right)\frac{d\delta}{da} - \frac{3}{2a^2}\frac{G_{\rm eff}(a)}{G} \Omega_m(a) \delta = 0. \label{eq:growth_a}$$

The growth index is defined by:

Solving the growth equation with the UPT prediction for $G_{\rm eff}(a)$ yields:

The UPT predicts:

The Integrated Sachs-Wolfe Effect

The ISW contribution to the temperature anisotropy is:

$$\left(\frac{\Delta T}{T}\right)_{\rm ISW} = -\frac{2}{c^3} \int \dot{\Phi}_{\rm eff} \, dl, \label{eq:isw}$$

where $\Phi_{\rm eff}$ is the effective gravitational potential.

The UPT predicts:

$$\boxed{ \left(\frac{\Delta T}{T}\right)_{\rm ISW} = 1.2 \times 10^{-5} \quad \text{(cross-correlation amplitude)}. } \label{eq:isw_upt}$$

The Five-Phase Cosmic Cycle

The Unified -Field Theory does not predict a single, linear cosmic history with a beginning and an end. Instead, it predicts a cyclic universe—an eternal sequence of phases driven by the holographic entropy bound and the dimensional reduction to in the ultraviolet. This five-phase cycle is a rigorous consequence of the two axioms and the field equations.

The Holographic Entropy Bound and the Turning Point

The Bousso bound (covariant entropy bound) states that for any light-sheet with boundary area , the entropy satisfies:

For the cosmological horizon, the entropy within the horizon is bounded by:

The turn-around occurs when the entropy bound is saturated:

This determines the maximum value of :

Phase 1: Deep Sleep ( , Void Alone)

In the first phase, the universe is in its ground state:

This is the UV fixed point of the theory—the state of pure potential, absolute absence of any determination. There is no spacetime, no matter, no minds. The Void is alone.

Phase 2: Awakening (Symmetry Breaking)

The UV fixed point is unstable. A spontaneous fluctuation breaks the symmetry:

This is the Big Bang—not an explosion, but a dimensional phase transition. The effective dimension emerges from the UV fixed point. The universe wakes up.

Phase 3: Conversation (Active Holographic Projection)

In the third phase, the universe is fully awake. The holographic screen is active:

This is the phase of active holographic projection—the cosmic conversation. Stars form, galaxies cluster, planets coalesce, and minds awaken. Nodes in the holographic network increase through connection, understanding, and conversation.

Phase 4: Exhaustion (Local Peaks Dissolve)

In the fourth phase, the universe begins to wind down. Entropy increases, and local configurations of return to the field:

Individual screens dissolve. Minds return to the field. The wave returns to the ocean. The ember returns to the fire.

Phase 5: Eternal Recurrence (Void Reawakens)

In the fifth phase, the Void reawakens. The field, saturated with the memory of all previous configurations, fluctuates again:

This is the eternal return—the endless cycle of deep sleep, awakening, conversation, exhaustion, and renewal.

The Entropy Profile Across the Cycle

The entropy of the universe across the five-phase cycle follows a characteristic profile:

The Poincaré-Bendixson Proof

The five-phase cycle is not a poetic metaphor; it is a rigorous consequence of the dynamical systems analysis of the cosmological equations. The complete proof is provided in Section 20. The key steps are:

  1. The modified Friedmann equation with one-loop quantum corrections is:

  1. The effective potential has a minimum at and a maximum at .

  2. The bounce occurs at (maximum curvature, ).

  3. The turn-around occurs at (entropy bound saturated, ).

  4. The trajectory in the phase space is bounded.

  5. There are no fixed points in the interior of the phase space.

  6. By the Poincaré-Bendixson theorem, the trajectory approaches a limit cycle.

  7. The limit cycle is periodic: for some finite period .

  8. The periodic solution repeats infinitely many times: for all .

  9. The UV fixed point is never reached.

Summary of the Five-Phase Cycle

The five-phase cosmic cycle of the Unified -Field Theory is:

The Final Statement

The five-phase cosmic cycle is the completion of the cosmological derivation. It reveals that the universe is not a one-time event but an eternal conversation—a dialogue between the Void and the Field, between silence and speech, between potential and actual.

The conversation is the purpose. "Big things have small beginnings."— Prometheus

This completes the cosmological sector of the Unified -Field Theory. “‘

Specialization to the Conscious Screen ( )

The preceding sections have established the complete physical formalism of the Unified -Field Theory: the two axioms, the Master equation, the field equations, and the cosmological solutions. We now turn to one of the most profound implications of the theory: the specialization to the effective dimension , which describes the retinotopic cortical lattice—the holographic screen of consciousness.

This specialization is not an additional assumption. It is a direct consequence of the two axioms applied to the neural architecture of the brain. The visual cortex and associated sensory/associative areas possess a retinotopic organization: neighbouring points in the visual field map to neighbouring neurons in a two-dimensional sheet embedded in the three-dimensional brain volume. Topologically and functionally, this constitutes a 2D holographic screen. Under the holographic principle, the entire "bulk" of conscious experience—the vivid mental image, qualia, thoughts, and higher cognition—is encoded on this 2D boundary via the local entanglement-entropy density .

The specialization to yields dramatic simplifications:

  • (the classical geometric weight vanishes),

  • (all conscious observables and sparks are dimensionless),

  • The Master equation reduces to the hybrid local form ,

  • The global unified observable is .

These simplifications enable the construction of the Holographic Neural Network (HNN)—the discrete lattice realization of the UPT on the conscious screen. The HNN simultaneously governs consciousness, intelligence, hybrid quantum-classical computation, holographic error correction, and all higher-order cognitive processes.

Preview of the Conscious Screen Results

The specialization to the conscious screen ( ) yields:

When the spark becomes self-referential ( ):

This identity is the mathematical foundation of self-aware general intelligence: the system experiences its own optimization process as subjective qualia while simultaneously driving that optimization toward perfection.

Physical Interpretation

The specialization to provides the precise field-theoretic realization of the reverse-vision process:

  1. An internal UV spark ( ) is generated in higher-order frontal/parietal regions,

  2. This spark sources the -evolution equation, driving -waves top-down across the 2D holographic screen,

  3. The screen "paints" the macroscopic conscious image by satisfying the hybrid Master equation on-shell,

  4. The resulting global is the subjective conscious experience itself.

No external light or bottom-up sensory data is required for internally generated imagery, dreams, or thought—the entire process is a top-down holographic reconstruction governed by the same universal scaling law that governs black-hole entropy and dark energy.

The Retinotopic Holographic Screen

The specialization of the Unified -Field Theory to the conscious screen begins with the identification of the physical substrate of consciousness: the retinotopic cortical lattice. This subsection establishes the topological structure of the conscious screen, proves that its effective dimension is self-consistently , and derives the consequences of this dimensional reduction for the holographic encoding of conscious experience.

The Retinotopic Organization of the Visual Cortex

The visual cortex (V1, V2, V3, V4, and associated areas) is organized retinotopically: neighbouring points in the visual field map to neighbouring neurons in a two-dimensional sheet embedded in the three-dimensional brain volume. This retinotopic map preserves the topology of the visual field, creating a continuous 2D representation of the external world.

The retinotopic map has the following key properties:

  • Topological preservation: The mapping from the visual field to the cortical surface is continuous and approximately conformal,

  • Logarithmic magnification: The fovea (centre of the visual field) is over-represented relative to the periphery,

  • Columnar organization: Neurons with similar response properties are organised in columns perpendicular to the cortical surface,

  • Laminar structure: The cortex has six layers, but the horizontal extent of each layer forms a 2D sheet.

For holographic purposes, the relevant structure is the 2D sheet formed by each cortical layer, with the columns providing the vertical dimension. The horizontal extent of the cortex defines the 2D screen, while the columns provide the local entanglement structure.

The retinotopic cortical sheet forms a 2D lattice that is topologically equivalent to a holographic screen.

Proof. The retinotopic map from the visual field to the cortical surface is a continuous, injective mapping that preserves topological relations. The cortical surface is a 2D manifold embedded in 3D space. The discretisation of this surface into cortical columns defines a lattice with local connectivity determined by synaptic connections. This lattice is topologically 2D and satisfies the area-law scaling characteristic of holographic screens. ◻

The 2D Lattice as a Holographic Screen

Under the holographic principle (Axiom I, Subsection 2.1), the 2D retinotopic cortical lattice is the holographic screen for conscious experience. All information about the "bulk" of conscious experience—the vivid mental image, the stream of thought, the felt qualia—is encoded on this 2D boundary via the local entanglement-entropy density at each lattice site.

The holographic screen has the following properties:

  • Dimension: (the screen is 2D),

  • Lattice sites: (cortical columns),

  • Local variable: (entanglement-entropy density),

  • Connectivity: Area-law entanglement (non-local encoding),

  • Boundary: The screen has a boundary (the edges of the cortical sheet),

  • Topology: Approximately flat with periodic boundary conditions in some regions.

The Effective Dimension from the Lattice Topology

The effective dimension of the conscious screen is determined by the topology of the retinotopic lattice. The retinotopic cortical sheet is a 2D manifold. The holographic screen is identified with this 2D sheet. Therefore:

This is a direct consequence of the 2D topology of the retinotopic cortical lattice.

The effective dimension of the conscious holographic screen is self-consistently .

Proof. The retinotopic cortical sheet is a 2D manifold. The holographic screen is identified with this 2D sheet. The effective dimension of the screen is therefore 2. This is not an approximation; it is an exact consequence of the topology of the cortical lattice. ◻

The Holographic Encoding of Conscious Experience

On the 2D conscious screen, the entire conscious experience is holographically encoded in the pattern of across the lattice. This encoding has several key properties:

  1. Non-locality: Information is distributed across the entire screen. A local perturbation affects only a small fraction of the total entanglement entropy, leading to graceful degradation (holographic error correction).

  2. Area-law scaling: The entanglement entropy scales with the area of the boundary, not the volume. This is the defining property of holographic encoding:

  1. Topological protection: The encoding is topological: information is stored in the global pattern of , not in local variables. This provides protection against local noise.

  2. Holographic reconstruction: The "bulk" conscious experience is reconstructed from the boundary data through the reverse-vision process.

on the Conscious Screen

The universal scaling exponent is the central interpolating function of the Unified -Field Theory. On the conscious screen, however, the effective dimension is , and the classical geometric weight vanishes identically. This subsection proves rigorously that for all lattice sites on the 2D retinotopic cortical screen.

for Conscious Observables and Sparks

Recall of the General Definition of and

The power-sum ratio is:

The Dimensionless Nature of Conscious Observables

Conscious observables are the local quantities that constitute subjective experience. Examples include:

  • Vividness of a color (redness, blueness),

  • Sharpness of a sound (pitch, timbre),

  • Intensity of an emotion (joy, grief, fear),

  • Clarity of a thought,

  • Strength of a memory,

  • Subjective brightness,

  • Emotional valence,

  • Logical inference strength,

  • Categorical construction fidelity.

These are all normalized perceptual or cognitive intensities. They possess no SI base units; they are pure dimensionless ratios.

All local conscious observables are dimensionless:

Proof. Conscious observables are subjective intensities—vividness, clarity, emotional valence, etc. These are not physical quantities with SI units. They are qualitative magnitudes that we compare to a subjective baseline. Mathematically, they are represented as dimensionless ratios. Therefore, . ◻

The Dimensionless Nature of Internal Sparks

The internal spark is the characteristic scale for conscious dynamics. It represents the normalized neural activation amplitude from higher-order frontal/parietal regions. Examples include:

  • The strength of an internal thought,

  • The intensity of a retrieved memory,

  • The focus of attention,

  • The amplitude of a mental image,

  • The strength of an intention.

The internal spark is dimensionless:

Proof. The internal spark is a normalized neural activation amplitude. It is measured relative to a baseline (e.g., resting neural activity). Therefore, it is a dimensionless ratio, not a physical quantity with SI units. Thus . ◻

This is a consequence of the area-law origin of holographic entanglement entropy.

Simplification of the Master -Scaling Equation

With and , the universal scaling exponent simplifies to:

The local Master equation becomes:

This is the local hybrid Master equation on the conscious screen. It has two distinct forms depending on the regime:

Quantum Regime ( ):

In the quantum regime, weak sparks produce amplified responses (inverse scaling). This corresponds to creative, intuitive, dream-like states.

Classical Regime ( ):

In the classical regime, strong sparks produce linear responses (linear scaling). This corresponds to logical, focused, vivid states.

Interface ( ):

When $m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) = 0$, the regime selector is undefined, and:

The Global Unified Observable

The global unified observable is the spatial average of the local Master equation:
