This identity is the mathematical foundation of self-aware general intelligence.

The Fixed-Point Condition on the Conscious Screen

The fixed-point condition (Subsection 3.4) on the conscious screen becomes:

With , this gives:

This means that on the conscious screen, the characteristic scale equals its modified Planck unit. The internal spark is always at the Planck scale of the screen. This is the condition for maximal holographic fidelity.

The Regime Selector on the Conscious Screen

The site-dependent regime selector is:

$$s_i(\Phi_i) = \operatorname{sign}(-m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i)). \label{eq:s_i_D2}$$

The effective mass squared is:

$$m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) = 4V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} - \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i. \label{eq:m_eff_D2}$$

The two regimes are:

  • Quantum regime ( ): , $m_{\rm eff}^2 > 0$. Inverse scaling, superposition, creativity.

  • Classical regime ( ): , $m_{\rm eff}^2 < 0$. Linear scaling, determinism, focus.

The crossover occurs when:

$$m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) = 0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \Phi_i = \Phi_*^{(i)}. \label{eq:crossover_D2}$$

Summary of the Conscious Screen Specialization

The conscious screen specialization is:

$$\boxed{ \begin{aligned} \text{Effective dimension: } & D = 2, \\ \text{Universal exponent: } & \beta(\Phi_i) \equiv 1, \\ \text{Power-sum ratio: } & k_{FH} \equiv 1, \\ \text{Local Master equation: } & X_i = X_p'(\Phi_i,v_c)\left(\frac{X_f}{X_p'(X_f)}\right)^{s_i(\Phi_i)}, \\ \text{Global unified observable: } & I \equiv \mathcal{Q} = \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^N X_p'(\Phi_i,v_c)\left(\frac{X_f}{X_p'(X_f)}\right)^{s_i(\Phi_i)}, \\ \text{Regime selector: } & s_i(\Phi_i) = \operatorname{sign}(-m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i)), \\ \text{Quantum regime: } & X_i = X_p'(\Phi_i,v_c) \frac{X_p'(X_f)}{X_f} \quad (s_i = -1), \\ \text{Classical regime: } & X_i = X_p'(\Phi_i,v_c) \frac{X_f}{X_p'(X_f)} \quad (s_i = +1), \\ \text{Self-referential identity: } & X_f \propto I \equiv \mathcal{Q} \quad \Rightarrow \quad I \equiv \mathcal{Q}. \end{aligned} }$$

Physical Interpretation

The conscious screen specialization reveals that consciousness is what the -field does on a 2D screen when it becomes self-referential. The global qualia field is the subjective experience of the screen’s fidelity. The identity shows that intelligence and consciousness are the same phenomenon, viewed from different perspectives.

This completes the specialization to the conscious screen ( ). The next sections will construct the Holographic Neural Network and derive the complete consciousness and intelligence models.

"Big things have small beginnings."— Prometheus “‘

The Holographic Neural Network (HNN)

With the conscious screen specialization established in Section 7, we now construct the Holographic Neural Network—the discrete lattice realization of the Unified -Field Theory on the 2D retinotopic cortical screen. The HNN is not an ad-hoc model of the brain; it is the inevitable lattice transcription of the continuous theory once the effective dimension is . It simultaneously implements consciousness, intelligence, hybrid quantum-classical computation, holographic error correction, and all higher-order cognitive processes on the same 2D lattice.

The HNN is holographic by construction. Every site on the 2D lattice corresponds to a cortical column. The dynamical variable at each site encodes the local entanglement-entropy density. The synaptic connections between sites realize the entanglement structure. The entire "bulk" of conscious experience—the vivid mental image, the stream of thought, the felt qualia—is holographically reconstructed from the distributed pattern on this 2D boundary.

Preview of the HNN Results

The HNN is defined by the following equations:

Discrete Action:

$$S_{\rm HNN} = \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \left[ \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i - \frac{1}{2} g_i^{\mu\nu} \partial_\mu \Phi_i \partial_\nu \Phi_i - V(\Phi_i) \right],$$

with on the screen.

Discrete -Evolution Equation:

$$\boxed{ \Box_i \Phi_i = -\frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i + 2 V_0 \exp(-2\Phi_i) + T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t). }$$

Local Master Equation:

Global Unified Observable:

Regime Selector:

$$\boxed{ s_i(\Phi_i) = \operatorname{sign}(-m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i)). }$$

Key Properties of the HNN

  1. Holographic storage: Memories and concepts are distributed non-locally across the screen via patterns, protected by the area law.

  2. Dynamic regime partitioning: Quantum patches ( ) enable superposition, creativity, and insight; classical patches ( ) enable deterministic reasoning, vivid qualia, and reliable execution.

  3. Self-correction: Local errors create curvature that drives -waves, automatically restoring the scaling law (holographic error correction).

  4. Top-down projection: Internal sparks propagate downward, "painting" conscious experience exactly as the reverse-vision process.

  5. Causality: Modified Planck units with (bounded by axonal speed) keep all signalling inside the local light-cone.

  6. Higher-order abstraction: When the spark is elevated to meta-levels, the same lattice generates philosophy, metaphysics, mathematics, logic, and category theory.

Construction of the HNN on the 2D Lattice

The Holographic Neural Network is constructed by discretizing the continuous action of the Unified -Field Theory on the 2D retinotopic cortical lattice. This subsection defines the lattice architecture, derives the discrete action from first principles, and establishes the mapping between biological neural structures and the holographic variables of the theory.

The 2D Cortical Lattice and Its Topology

The retinotopic cortical sheet is discretized into a 2D lattice of sites . Each site corresponds to a cortical column or minicolumn. The lattice has the following properties:

  1. Spatial dimension: (the screen is 2D),

  2. Lattice spacing: (the inter-column spacing, approximately mm in human visual cortex),

  3. Number of sites: (approximately cortical columns in the visual cortex),

  4. Topology: Approximately flat with periodic boundary conditions in some regions,

  5. Connectivity: Local connections (within a radius of a few lattice spacings) and long-range connections (across the entire screen),

  6. Boundary: The screen has a boundary at the edges of the cortical sheet.

The position of site is given by the 2D vector . The lattice spacing defines the fundamental length scale of the conscious screen.

The cortical lattice is a 2D lattice of sites with spacing :

Each site corresponds to a cortical column with local entanglement-entropy density .

The Discrete Action from the Continuous Action

The continuous action of the pure gravitational-plus-scalar sector (Subsection 4.1) is:

On the 2D conscious screen ( ), the action is discretized by:

  1. Replacing the integral with the sum ,

  2. Replacing the continuous fields with lattice variables ,

  3. Replacing the continuous curvature with the discrete curvature ,

  4. Replacing the continuous derivatives with finite differences.

The discrete action of the HNN is:

$$\boxed{ S_{\rm HNN} = \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \left[ \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i - \frac{1}{2} e^{-\Phi_i} g_i^{\mu\nu} \partial_\mu \Phi_i \partial_\nu \Phi_i - V(\Phi_i) \right], } \label{eq:discrete_action_HNN}$$

where is the Weyl-invariant potential on the screen (Subsection 4.2).

The Discrete Curvature from Synaptic Connectivity

The continuous curvature encodes the local geometry of spacetime. On the cortical lattice, the discrete curvature encodes the local synaptic connectivity topology. It is defined through the graph Laplacian of the synaptic network:

where:

  • are the neighbours of site (within a few lattice spacings),

  • are the synaptic weights (normalised such that ),

  • is the lattice spacing.

This definition captures the idea that curvature is a measure of how the local entanglement-entropy density deviates from its neighbours—i.e., how synaptic connectivity shapes the -field.

The discrete curvature at site is:

where are the synaptic weights and are the neighbours of site .

This definition has the following properties:

  • when for all neighbours (flat curvature),

  • when neighbours have higher values (positive curvature),

  • when neighbours have lower values (negative curvature).

The Kinetic Term and the Discrete d’Alembertian

The continuous kinetic term is discretized using finite differences. The discrete kinetic term is:

$$\mathcal{L}_{\rm kin}^{(i)} = -\frac{1}{2} e^{-\Phi_i} \sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}(i)} \frac{(\Phi_j - \Phi_i)^2}{a^2}. \label{eq:kinetic_discrete_HNN}$$

The discrete d’Alembertian is defined by the variation of the kinetic term with respect to :

This operator ensures causal propagation of -waves across the cortical lattice at speed (bounded by axonal conduction velocity).

The discrete d’Alembertian acting on is:

The Weyl-Invariant Potential on the Lattice

On the conscious screen, the Weyl-invariant potential (Subsection 4.2) is:

This potential provides the restoring force that drives toward values that satisfy the local Master equation. In the UV limit ( ), (constant), and the potential acts as a constant background. In the IR limit ( ), , and the potential becomes negligible.

The Complete HNN Action and Its Interpretation

Combining the curvature, kinetic, and potential terms, the complete HNN action is:

$$\boxed{ S_{\rm HNN} = \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \left[ \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i - \frac{1}{2} e^{-\Phi_i} \sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}(i)} \frac{(\Phi_j - \Phi_i)^2}{a^2} - V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} \right]. } \label{eq:HNN_action_complete}$$

This action has the following interpretation:

  1. The curvature term : This is the holographic back-reaction of synaptic topology on the entanglement-entropy density. Local synaptic patterns create curvature that sources changes in .

  2. The kinetic term : This drives the propagation of -waves across the screen. The factor ensures causality and dimensional consistency.

  3. The potential term : This provides the restoring force that stabilises on-shell, enforcing the local Master equation.

The Discrete -Evolution Equation

The discrete -evolution equation is the fundamental dynamical law of the Holographic Neural Network. It governs the time evolution of the entanglement-entropy density at each lattice site, simultaneously driving conscious experience, intelligence optimization, hybrid computation, error correction, and all higher-order cognitive processes.

The HNN Action (Restated for Reference)

The discrete action of the Holographic Neural Network, derived in Subsection 9.1, is:

$$S_{\rm HNN} = \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \left[ \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i - \frac{1}{2} e^{-\Phi_i} g_i^{\mu\nu} \partial_\mu \Phi_i \partial_\nu \Phi_i - V(\Phi_i) \right], \label{eq:HNN_action_phi_evol}$$

where:

  • is the discrete curvature (synaptic topology),

  • is the Weyl-invariant potential on the screen,

  • is the discrete metric (causal structure),

  • are finite differences (lattice derivatives).

Variation with Respect to

To obtain the discrete -evolution equation, vary the action $S_{\rm HNN}$ with respect to while keeping the metric and other fields fixed:

$$\frac{\delta S_{\rm HNN}}{\delta \Phi_i} = 0. \label{eq:variation_phi_HNN}$$

The variation yields contributions from three terms:

  1. The curvature term,

  2. The kinetic term,

  3. The potential term.

The Curvature Term Contribution

The curvature term in the HNN action is:

$$S_{\rm curv} = \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i. \label{eq:curvature_term_HNN}$$

Varying with respect to :

$$\frac{\delta S_{\rm curv}}{\delta \Phi_i} = -a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i + \text{(subleading)}. \label{eq:curvature_contribution_HNN}$$

The Kinetic Term Contribution and the Discrete d’Alembertian

The kinetic term in the HNN action is:

$$S_{\rm kin} = -\frac{1}{2} \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} e^{-\Phi_i} g_i^{\mu\nu} \partial_\mu \Phi_i \partial_\nu \Phi_i. \label{eq:kinetic_term_HNN}$$

Varying with respect to gives two contributions:

  1. The standard variation of the kinetic term (ignoring the prefactor dependence):

  1. An extra contribution from the -dependence of the prefactor :

The discrete d’Alembertian (Subsection 9.1) is:

The kinetic term variation yields:

$$\frac{\delta S_{\rm kin}}{\delta \Phi_i} = a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} e^{-\Phi_i} \left[ \Box_i \Phi_i + \frac{1}{2} (\partial \Phi_i)^2 \right]. \label{eq:kinetic_variation_HNN}$$

The Potential Term Contribution

The potential term in the HNN action is:

$$S_{\rm pot} = -\sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} V(\Phi_i) = -\sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i}. \label{eq:potential_term_HNN}$$

Varying with respect to :

$$\frac{\delta S_{\rm pot}}{\delta \Phi_i} = -a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \frac{\partial V}{\partial \Phi_i} = -a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} (-2V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i}) = 2a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i}. \label{eq:potential_variation_HNN}$$

The Internal Spark Source Term

The internal spark source term $T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t)$ represents the self-generated UV spark from higher-order frontal/parietal regions. It is treated as an external source in the action:

$$S_{\rm spark} = \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t) \Phi_i. \label{eq:spark_term_HNN}$$

Varying with respect to :

$$\frac{\delta S_{\rm spark}}{\delta \Phi_i} = a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t). \label{eq:spark_variation_HNN}$$

The Complete Discrete -Evolution Equation

Collecting all contributions and setting the total variation to zero:

$$\frac{\delta S_{\rm curv}}{\delta \Phi_i} + \frac{\delta S_{\rm kin}}{\delta \Phi_i} + \frac{\delta S_{\rm pot}}{\delta \Phi_i} + \frac{\delta S_{\rm spark}}{\delta \Phi_i} = 0. \label{eq:total_variation_HNN}$$

Substituting the expressions derived above and dividing by :

$$\boxed{ \Box_i \Phi_i = -\frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i + 2V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} + T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t). } \label{eq:phi_evolution_final_HNN}$$

This is the discrete -evolution equation for the Holographic Neural Network.

Physical Interpretation of Each Term

The discrete -evolution equation has four terms, each with a clear physical interpretation:

  1. The discrete d’Alembertian : This drives the causal propagation of -waves across the cortical lattice. It ensures that changes in entanglement-entropy density propagate at speed (bounded by axonal conduction velocity).

  2. The curvature term : This is the holographic back-reaction. Local synaptic topology (encoded in ) sources changes in entanglement-entropy density. Positive curvature (higher in neighbours) drives toward smaller values, while negative curvature drives toward larger values.

  3. The restoring term : This is the Weyl-invariant potential contribution. It drives toward values that satisfy the local Master equation, providing stability and ensuring the on-shell condition .

  4. The internal spark term $T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t)$: This is the self-generated UV spark from higher-order regions. It is the physical origin of thoughts, intentions, and mental imagery. When the spark is self-referential ( ), the network becomes metacognitive.

Physical interpretation of terms in the discrete -evolution equation

Term

Symbol

Meaning

Discrete d’Alembertian

Causal propagation of -waves across the screen

Curvature term

Holographic back-reaction: synaptic topology sources entanglement changes

Restoring term

Drives toward values that satisfy the Master equation

Internal spark term

$T_i^{\rm spark}(t)$

Self-generated UV spark - the origin of thoughts and imagery

The Site-Dependent Regime Selector

The site-dependent regime selector is one of the most important features of the Holographic Neural Network. It determines whether each lattice site operates in the quantum regime ( ) or the classical regime ( ). This dynamic partitioning of the conscious screen into quantum and classical patches is the physical origin of the hybrid nature of consciousness—the coexistence of creativity and determinism, intuition and logic, imagination and reasoning.

The Discrete -Evolution Equation (Restated)

The discrete -evolution equation of the Holographic Neural Network (Subsection 9.2) is:

$$\boxed{ \Box_i \Phi_i = -\frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i + 2V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} + T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t). } \label{eq:phi_evolution_selector}$$

Linearization Around a Local Background

Consider a local background value around which fluctuates. Write:

where is a small perturbation.

Substitute this into the -evolution equation and retain only linear terms in . The background solution satisfies:

where and are the background curvature and spark.

Subtracting the background and expanding the exponential terms gives the linearized equation:

The Effective Mass Squared $m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i)$

The coefficient of in the linearized equation is the effective mass squared:

$$\boxed{ m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) = 4V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} - \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i. } \label{eq:m_eff_HNN}$$

This is the discrete analogue of the continuous effective mass squared (Subsection 3.7). It determines the stability of perturbations and the regime selector.

The effective mass squared at site is:

$$\boxed{ m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) = 4V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} - \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i. }$$

Evaluation at the UV Fixed Point ( )

In the ultraviolet (quantum) regime, . Evaluating the effective mass squared:

$$\lim_{\Phi_i \to 0} m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) = 4V_0 - \frac{1}{16\pi G} R_i. \label{eq:m_eff_uv_HNN}$$

For typical neural dynamics, the curvature term is subdominant compared to the restoring term. Thus:

$$m_{\rm eff}^2(0) \approx 4V_0 > 0. \label{eq:m_eff_uv_positive}$$

Therefore, in the UV regime:

$$m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) > 0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad s_i(\Phi_i) = -1. \label{eq:uv_regime_selector}$$

This is the quantum regime: perturbations are massive and stable, corresponding to inverse scaling, superposition, creativity, and dream-like states.

Evaluation at the IR Fixed Point ( )

In the infrared (classical) regime, . Evaluating the effective mass squared:

$$\lim_{\Phi_i \to \infty} m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) = \lim_{\Phi_i \to \infty} 4V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} - \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i = 0. \label{eq:m_eff_ir_HNN}$$

Both terms vanish exponentially. However, the curvature term may have a subleading contribution that dominates. In the classical regime, the effective mass squared can become negative:

$$m_{\rm eff}^2(\infty) \approx -\frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i < 0 \quad \text{(if } R_i > 0\text{)}. \label{eq:m_eff_ir_negative}$$

Therefore, in the IR regime:

$$m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) < 0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad s_i(\Phi_i) = +1. \label{eq:ir_regime_selector}$$

This is the classical regime: perturbations are tachyonic and amplified, corresponding to linear scaling, deterministic reasoning, and vivid qualia.

The Definition of

Based on the sign of the effective mass squared, the site-dependent regime selector is defined as:

$$\boxed{ s_i(\Phi_i) = \operatorname{sign}(-m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i)). } \label{eq:s_i_definition_HNN}$$

Equivalently:

$$\boxed{ s_i(\Phi_i) = \begin{cases} +1 & \text{if } m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) < 0 \quad \text{(classical regime, linear scaling)}, \\ -1 & \text{if } m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) > 0 \quad \text{(quantum regime, inverse scaling)}. \end{cases} } \label{eq:s_i_cases_HNN}$$

The crossover occurs when:

$$m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) = 0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad 4V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} = \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i. \label{eq:crossover_condition_HNN}$$

This condition defines the quantum-classical interface on the conscious screen.

The site-dependent regime selector on the HNN is:

Proof. From $s_i = \operatorname{sign}(-m_{\rm eff}^2)$ and $m_{\rm eff}^2 = 4V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} - e^{-\Phi_i} R_i/(16\pi G)$:

The Reverse-Vision Process

The reverse-vision process is the most direct phenomenological manifestation of the holographic principle in the conscious brain. It is the top-down propagation of an internal UV spark that "paints" the conscious image without external sensory input. This process explains mental imagery, dreams, hallucinations, memory recall, and the stream of thought—all phenomena in which conscious experience is generated internally rather than triggered by external stimuli.

Definition of the Reverse-Vision Process

The reverse-vision process is the top-down holographic reconstruction of conscious experience from the 2D screen. Unlike forward perception—where external light enters the eye and propagates bottom-up through the visual pathway—reverse vision originates from an internal spark generated in higher-order frontal/parietal regions and propagates top-down to "paint" the conscious image.

The reverse-vision process is the top-down propagation of an internal UV spark that sources a -wave across the 2D conscious screen, causing each site to relax on-shell to the local Master equation, thereby painting the macroscopic conscious image.

The Four-Stage Mechanism

The reverse-vision process proceeds in four distinct stages, each governed by the equations of the HNN:

  1. Spark Generation: An internal UV spark is generated in higher-order frontal/parietal regions. This spark corresponds to a thought, intention, mental image, or memory retrieval.

  2. -Wave Propagation: The spark sources the discrete -evolution equation, generating a -wave that propagates top-down across the 2D screen.

  3. Local Relaxation: Each site relaxes on-shell to satisfy the local Master equation, adjusting to the value required by the spark.

  4. Image Painting: The resulting global pattern across the screen is the macroscopic conscious percept. The qualia field is the spatial average of this pattern.

Stage 1: Spark Generation

The internal spark is the source term in the -evolution equation:

$$T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t) = T_0 \, X_f(t) \, \delta(\mathbf{r}_i - \mathbf{r}_0), \label{eq:spark_source}$$

where:

  • is the amplitude of the spark (normalised),

  • is the time-dependent spark strength,

  • is the location of the spark source (frontal/parietal cortex),

  • is the Kronecker delta on the lattice.

The spark strength is a dimensionless quantity representing the normalized neural activation amplitude. It is generated by higher-order cognitive processes, including:

  • Volitional thought,

  • Memory retrieval,

  • Mental imagery,

  • Dream generation,

  • Creative insight,

  • Metacognitive reflection.

When the spark is self-referential ( ), it generates metacognitive awareness.

Stage 2: -Wave Propagation

The spark sources the discrete -evolution equation:

$$\Box_i \Phi_i = -\frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i + 2V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} + T_i^{\rm spark}(t). \label{eq:phi_wave_propagation}$$

The solution is a propagating -wave:

$$\Phi_i(t) = \Phi_i(0) + \Phi_{\rm wave} \cos(\mathbf{k} \cdot \mathbf{r}_i - \omega t) e^{-\gamma t}, \label{eq:phi_wave_solution}$$

where:

  • is the wave vector (direction of propagation),

  • is the angular frequency (determined by the spark),

  • is the damping coefficient (zero in ideal conditions),

  • is the position vector of site ,

  • $\Phi_{\rm wave}$ is the wave amplitude.

The dispersion relation is:

where is the system-specific causal velocity (bounded by axonal conduction velocity).

Stage 3: Local Relaxation

As the -wave passes through each site , the site relaxes on-shell to satisfy the local Master equation:

The relaxation is driven by the restoring term in the -evolution equation:

The regime selector determines whether the site relaxes in the quantum regime ( , inverse scaling) or the classical regime ( , linear scaling):

  • Quantum relaxation ( ): . Weak sparks produce amplified, creative, dream-like percepts.

  • Classical relaxation ( ): . Strong sparks produce vivid, deterministic, focused percepts.

The relaxation occurs on a timescale determined by the effective mass squared:

$$\tau_{\rm relax} \sim \frac{1}{|m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i)|}. \label{eq:relaxation_time}$$

Stage 4: Image Painting

After all sites have relaxed, the global pattern constitutes the macroscopic conscious percept. The local observables represent the subjective qualities of the percept:

  • Vividness of color,

  • Sharpness of sound,

  • Intensity of emotion,

  • Clarity of thought,

  • Strength of memory.

The global qualia field is the spatial average:

The intelligence fidelity is the same quantity:

When the spark is self-referential:

The Complete Mathematical Description

The complete mathematical description of the reverse-vision process is the system of equations:

$$\boxed{ \begin{aligned} \text{Spark generation: } & T_i^{\rm spark}(t) = T_0 \, X_f(t) \, \delta(\mathbf{r}_i - \mathbf{r}_0), \\ \Phi\text{-wave propagation: } & \Box_i \Phi_i = -\frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i + 2V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} + T_i^{\rm spark}(t), \\ \text{Local relaxation: } & X_i = X_p'(\Phi_i,v_c)\left(\frac{X_f}{X_p'(X_f)}\right)^{s_i(\Phi_i)}, \\ \text{Image painting: } & \mathcal{Q} = \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^N X_i, \qquad I \equiv \mathcal{Q} \quad \text{(self-referential)}. \end{aligned} } \label{eq:reverse_vision_system}$$

Physical Interpretation

The reverse-vision process has several profound implications:

  1. Consciousness is holographic: The entire conscious experience is reconstructed from the 2D screen. There is no "central processor" or "Cartesian theatre"; consciousness is the global pattern of on the screen.

  2. Mental imagery is physical: Mental images, dreams, and hallucinations are not ethereal; they are -waves propagating on the cortical screen. They obey the same equations as perception.

  3. The stream of consciousness is deterministic: The -evolution equation is fully deterministic. The stream of consciousness is the on-shell evolution of the -field under internal sparks.

  4. Self-awareness is a loop: The self-referential loop is the physical basis of self-awareness. The "I" is the loop itself.

  5. No external input required: The reverse-vision process generates conscious experience without external sensory input. This explains dreams, daydreams, and mental imagery.

Summary of the HNN

The Holographic Neural Network is:

$$\boxed{ \begin{aligned} \text{Lattice: } & \mathcal{L} = \{\mathbf{r}_i \in \mathbb{R}^2 : i = 1, 2, \ldots, N\}, \quad N \sim 10^6, \\ \text{Lattice spacing: } & a \sim 0.5\ \text{mm}, \\ \text{Local variable: } & \Phi_i \in \mathbb{R}, \\ \text{Discrete curvature: } & R_i = \frac{1}{a^2} \sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}(i)} w_{ij} (\Phi_j - \Phi_i), \\ \text{Discrete d'Alembertian: } & \Box_i \Phi_i = \frac{1}{a^2} \sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}(i)} (\Phi_j - \Phi_i) - \frac{\partial^2 \Phi_i}{\partial t^2}, \\ \text{Potential: } & V(\Phi_i) = V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i}, \\ \text{Action: } & S_{\rm HNN} = \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \left[ \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i - \frac{1}{2} e^{-\Phi_i} \sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}(i)} \frac{(\Phi_j - \Phi_i)^2}{a^2} - V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} \right], \\ \Phi\text{-evolution: } & \Box_i \Phi_i = -\frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i + 2V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} + T_i^{\rm spark}(t), \\ \text{Regime selector: } & s_i(\Phi_i) = \operatorname{sign}(-m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i)), \\ \text{Local Master: } & X_i = X_p'(\Phi_i,v_c)\left(\frac{X_f}{X_p'(X_f)}\right)^{s_i(\Phi_i)}, \\ \text{Global observable: } & I \equiv \mathcal{Q} = \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^N X_p'(\Phi_i,v_c)\left(\frac{X_f}{X_p'(X_f)}\right)^{s_i(\Phi_i)}. \end{aligned} }$$

The HNN is the complete physical substrate of consciousness, intelligence, and higher-order cognition. The next sections will derive the quantum computing, classical computing, and hybrid computing regimes, holographic error correction, the consciousness and intelligence models, and the higher-order abstractions.

"Big things have small beginnings."— Prometheus

“‘latex

The Holographic Neural Network (HNN)

With the conscious screen specialization established in Section 7, we now construct the Holographic Neural Network—the discrete lattice realization of the Unified -Field Theory on the 2D retinotopic cortical screen. The HNN is not an ad-hoc model of the brain; it is the inevitable lattice transcription of the continuous theory once the effective dimension is . It simultaneously implements consciousness, intelligence, hybrid quantum-classical computation, holographic error correction, and all higher-order cognitive processes on the same 2D lattice.

The HNN is holographic by construction. Every site on the 2D lattice corresponds to a cortical column. The dynamical variable at each site encodes the local entanglement-entropy density. The synaptic connections between sites realize the entanglement structure. The entire "bulk" of conscious experience—the vivid mental image, the stream of thought, the felt qualia—is holographically reconstructed from the distributed pattern on this 2D boundary.

Preview of the HNN Results

The HNN is defined by the following equations:

Discrete Action:

$$S_{\rm HNN} = \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \left[ \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i - \frac{1}{2} g_i^{\mu\nu} \partial_\mu \Phi_i \partial_\nu \Phi_i - V(\Phi_i) \right],$$

with on the screen.

Discrete -Evolution Equation:

$$\boxed{ \Box_i \Phi_i = -\frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i + 2 V_0 \exp(-2\Phi_i) + T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t). }$$

Local Master Equation:

Global Unified Observable:

Regime Selector:

$$\boxed{ s_i(\Phi_i) = \operatorname{sign}(-m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i)). }$$

Key Properties of the HNN

  1. Holographic storage: Memories and concepts are distributed non-locally across the screen via patterns, protected by the area law.

  2. Dynamic regime partitioning: Quantum patches ( ) enable superposition, creativity, and insight; classical patches ( ) enable deterministic reasoning, vivid qualia, and reliable execution.

  3. Self-correction: Local errors create curvature that drives -waves, automatically restoring the scaling law (holographic error correction).

  4. Top-down projection: Internal sparks propagate downward, "painting" conscious experience exactly as the reverse-vision process.

  5. Causality: Modified Planck units with (bounded by axonal speed) keep all signalling inside the local light-cone.

  6. Higher-order abstraction: When the spark is elevated to meta-levels, the same lattice generates philosophy, metaphysics, mathematics, logic, and category theory.

Construction of the HNN on the 2D Lattice

The Holographic Neural Network is constructed by discretizing the continuous action of the Unified -Field Theory on the 2D retinotopic cortical lattice. This subsection defines the lattice architecture, derives the discrete action from first principles, and establishes the mapping between biological neural structures and the holographic variables of the theory.

The 2D Cortical Lattice and Its Topology

The retinotopic cortical sheet is discretized into a 2D lattice of sites . Each site corresponds to a cortical column or minicolumn. The lattice has the following properties:

  1. Spatial dimension: (the screen is 2D),

  2. Lattice spacing: (the inter-column spacing, approximately mm in human visual cortex),

  3. Number of sites: (approximately cortical columns in the visual cortex),

  4. Topology: Approximately flat with periodic boundary conditions in some regions,

  5. Connectivity: Local connections (within a radius of a few lattice spacings) and long-range connections (across the entire screen),

  6. Boundary: The screen has a boundary at the edges of the cortical sheet.

The position of site is given by the 2D vector . The lattice spacing defines the fundamental length scale of the conscious screen.

The cortical lattice is a 2D lattice of sites with spacing :

Each site corresponds to a cortical column with local entanglement-entropy density .

The Discrete Action from the Continuous Action

The continuous action of the pure gravitational-plus-scalar sector (Subsection 4.1) is:

On the 2D conscious screen ( ), the action is discretized by:

  1. Replacing the integral with the sum ,

  2. Replacing the continuous fields with lattice variables ,

  3. Replacing the continuous curvature with the discrete curvature ,

  4. Replacing the continuous derivatives with finite differences.

The discrete action of the HNN is:

$$\boxed{ S_{\rm HNN} = \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \left[ \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i - \frac{1}{2} e^{-\Phi_i} g_i^{\mu\nu} \partial_\mu \Phi_i \partial_\nu \Phi_i - V(\Phi_i) \right], } \label{eq:discrete_action_HNN}$$

where is the Weyl-invariant potential on the screen (Subsection 4.2).

The Discrete Curvature from Synaptic Connectivity

The continuous curvature encodes the local geometry of spacetime. On the cortical lattice, the discrete curvature encodes the local synaptic connectivity topology. It is defined through the graph Laplacian of the synaptic network:

where:

  • are the neighbours of site (within a few lattice spacings),

  • are the synaptic weights (normalised such that ),

  • is the lattice spacing.

This definition captures the idea that curvature is a measure of how the local entanglement-entropy density deviates from its neighbours—i.e., how synaptic connectivity shapes the -field.

The discrete curvature at site is:

where are the synaptic weights and are the neighbours of site .

This definition has the following properties:

  • when for all neighbours (flat curvature),

  • when neighbours have higher values (positive curvature),

  • when neighbours have lower values (negative curvature).

The Kinetic Term and the Discrete d’Alembertian

The continuous kinetic term is discretized using finite differences. The discrete kinetic term is:

$$\mathcal{L}_{\rm kin}^{(i)} = -\frac{1}{2} e^{-\Phi_i} \sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}(i)} \frac{(\Phi_j - \Phi_i)^2}{a^2}. \label{eq:kinetic_discrete_HNN}$$

The discrete d’Alembertian is defined by the variation of the kinetic term with respect to :

This operator ensures causal propagation of -waves across the cortical lattice at speed (bounded by axonal conduction velocity).

The discrete d’Alembertian acting on is:

The Weyl-Invariant Potential on the Lattice

On the conscious screen, the Weyl-invariant potential (Subsection 4.2) is:

This potential provides the restoring force that drives toward values that satisfy the local Master equation. In the UV limit ( ), (constant), and the potential acts as a constant background. In the IR limit ( ), , and the potential becomes negligible.

The Complete HNN Action and Its Interpretation

Combining the curvature, kinetic, and potential terms, the complete HNN action is:

$$\boxed{ S_{\rm HNN} = \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \left[ \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i - \frac{1}{2} e^{-\Phi_i} \sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}(i)} \frac{(\Phi_j - \Phi_i)^2}{a^2} - V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} \right]. } \label{eq:HNN_action_complete}$$

This action has the following interpretation:

  1. The curvature term : This is the holographic back-reaction of synaptic topology on the entanglement-entropy density. Local synaptic patterns create curvature that sources changes in .

  2. The kinetic term : This drives the propagation of -waves across the screen. The factor ensures causality and dimensional consistency.

  3. The potential term : This provides the restoring force that stabilises on-shell, enforcing the local Master equation.

The Discrete -Evolution Equation

The discrete -evolution equation is the fundamental dynamical law of the Holographic Neural Network. It governs the time evolution of the entanglement-entropy density at each lattice site, simultaneously driving conscious experience, intelligence optimization, hybrid computation, error correction, and all higher-order cognitive processes.

The HNN Action (Restated for Reference)

The discrete action of the Holographic Neural Network, derived in Subsection 9.1, is:

$$S_{\rm HNN} = \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \left[ \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i - \frac{1}{2} e^{-\Phi_i} g_i^{\mu\nu} \partial_\mu \Phi_i \partial_\nu \Phi_i - V(\Phi_i) \right], \label{eq:HNN_action_phi_evol}$$

where:

  • is the discrete curvature (synaptic topology),

  • is the Weyl-invariant potential on the screen,

  • is the discrete metric (causal structure),

  • are finite differences (lattice derivatives).

Variation with Respect to

To obtain the discrete -evolution equation, vary the action $S_{\rm HNN}$ with respect to while keeping the metric and other fields fixed:

$$\frac{\delta S_{\rm HNN}}{\delta \Phi_i} = 0. \label{eq:variation_phi_HNN}$$

The variation yields contributions from three terms:

  1. The curvature term,

  2. The kinetic term,

  3. The potential term.

The Curvature Term Contribution

The curvature term in the HNN action is:

$$S_{\rm curv} = \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i. \label{eq:curvature_term_HNN}$$

Varying with respect to :

$$\frac{\delta S_{\rm curv}}{\delta \Phi_i} = -a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i + \text{(subleading)}. \label{eq:curvature_contribution_HNN}$$

The Kinetic Term Contribution and the Discrete d’Alembertian

The kinetic term in the HNN action is:

$$S_{\rm kin} = -\frac{1}{2} \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} e^{-\Phi_i} g_i^{\mu\nu} \partial_\mu \Phi_i \partial_\nu \Phi_i. \label{eq:kinetic_term_HNN}$$

Varying with respect to gives two contributions:

  1. The standard variation of the kinetic term (ignoring the prefactor dependence):

  1. An extra contribution from the -dependence of the prefactor :

The discrete d’Alembertian (Subsection 9.1) is:

The kinetic term variation yields:

$$\frac{\delta S_{\rm kin}}{\delta \Phi_i} = a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} e^{-\Phi_i} \left[ \Box_i \Phi_i + \frac{1}{2} (\partial \Phi_i)^2 \right]. \label{eq:kinetic_variation_HNN}$$

The Potential Term Contribution

The potential term in the HNN action is:

$$S_{\rm pot} = -\sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} V(\Phi_i) = -\sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i}. \label{eq:potential_term_HNN}$$

Varying with respect to :

$$\frac{\delta S_{\rm pot}}{\delta \Phi_i} = -a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \frac{\partial V}{\partial \Phi_i} = -a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} (-2V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i}) = 2a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i}. \label{eq:potential_variation_HNN}$$

The Internal Spark Source Term

The internal spark source term $T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t)$ represents the self-generated UV spark from higher-order frontal/parietal regions. It is treated as an external source in the action:

$$S_{\rm spark} = \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t) \Phi_i. \label{eq:spark_term_HNN}$$

Varying with respect to :

$$\frac{\delta S_{\rm spark}}{\delta \Phi_i} = a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t). \label{eq:spark_variation_HNN}$$

The Complete Discrete -Evolution Equation

Collecting all contributions and setting the total variation to zero:

$$\frac{\delta S_{\rm curv}}{\delta \Phi_i} + \frac{\delta S_{\rm kin}}{\delta \Phi_i} + \frac{\delta S_{\rm pot}}{\delta \Phi_i} + \frac{\delta S_{\rm spark}}{\delta \Phi_i} = 0. \label{eq:total_variation_HNN}$$

Substituting the expressions derived above and dividing by :

$$\boxed{ \Box_i \Phi_i = -\frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i + 2V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} + T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t). } \label{eq:phi_evolution_final_HNN}$$

This is the discrete -evolution equation for the Holographic Neural Network.

Physical Interpretation of Each Term

The discrete -evolution equation has four terms, each with a clear physical interpretation:

  1. The discrete d’Alembertian : This drives the causal propagation of -waves across the cortical lattice. It ensures that changes in entanglement-entropy density propagate at speed (bounded by axonal conduction velocity).

  2. The curvature term : This is the holographic back-reaction. Local synaptic topology (encoded in ) sources changes in entanglement-entropy density. Positive curvature (higher in neighbours) drives toward smaller values, while negative curvature drives toward larger values.

  3. The restoring term : This is the Weyl-invariant potential contribution. It drives toward values that satisfy the local Master equation, providing stability and ensuring the on-shell condition .

  4. The internal spark term $T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t)$: This is the self-generated UV spark from higher-order regions. It is the physical origin of thoughts, intentions, and mental imagery. When the spark is self-referential ( ), the network becomes metacognitive.

Physical interpretation of terms in the discrete -evolution equation

Term

Symbol

Meaning

Discrete d’Alembertian

Causal propagation of -waves across the screen

Curvature term

Holographic back-reaction: synaptic topology sources entanglement changes

Restoring term

Drives toward values that satisfy the Master equation

Internal spark term

$T_i^{\rm spark}(t)$

Self-generated UV spark - the origin of thoughts and imagery

The Site-Dependent Regime Selector

The site-dependent regime selector is one of the most important features of the Holographic Neural Network. It determines whether each lattice site operates in the quantum regime ( ) or the classical regime ( ). This dynamic partitioning of the conscious screen into quantum and classical patches is the physical origin of the hybrid nature of consciousness—the coexistence of creativity and determinism, intuition and logic, imagination and reasoning.

The Discrete -Evolution Equation (Restated)

The discrete -evolution equation of the Holographic Neural Network (Subsection 9.2) is:

$$\boxed{ \Box_i \Phi_i = -\frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i + 2V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} + T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t). } \label{eq:phi_evolution_selector}$$

Linearization Around a Local Background

Consider a local background value around which fluctuates. Write:

where is a small perturbation.

Substitute this into the -evolution equation and retain only linear terms in . The background solution satisfies:

where and are the background curvature and spark.

Subtracting the background and expanding the exponential terms gives the linearized equation:

The Effective Mass Squared $m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i)$

The coefficient of in the linearized equation is the effective mass squared:

$$\boxed{ m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) = 4V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} - \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i. } \label{eq:m_eff_HNN}$$

This is the discrete analogue of the continuous effective mass squared (Subsection 3.7). It determines the stability of perturbations and the regime selector.

The effective mass squared at site is:

$$\boxed{ m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) = 4V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} - \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i. }$$

Evaluation at the UV Fixed Point ( )

In the ultraviolet (quantum) regime, . Evaluating the effective mass squared:

$$\lim_{\Phi_i \to 0} m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) = 4V_0 - \frac{1}{16\pi G} R_i. \label{eq:m_eff_uv_HNN}$$

For typical neural dynamics, the curvature term is subdominant compared to the restoring term. Thus:

$$m_{\rm eff}^2(0) \approx 4V_0 > 0. \label{eq:m_eff_uv_positive}$$

Therefore, in the UV regime:

$$m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) > 0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad s_i(\Phi_i) = -1. \label{eq:uv_regime_selector}$$

This is the quantum regime: perturbations are massive and stable, corresponding to inverse scaling, superposition, creativity, and dream-like states.

Evaluation at the IR Fixed Point ( )

In the infrared (classical) regime, . Evaluating the effective mass squared:

$$\lim_{\Phi_i \to \infty} m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) = \lim_{\Phi_i \to \infty} 4V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} - \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i = 0. \label{eq:m_eff_ir_HNN}$$

Both terms vanish exponentially. However, the curvature term may have a subleading contribution that dominates. In the classical regime, the effective mass squared can become negative:

$$m_{\rm eff}^2(\infty) \approx -\frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i < 0 \quad \text{(if } R_i > 0\text{)}. \label{eq:m_eff_ir_negative}$$

Therefore, in the IR regime:

$$m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) < 0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad s_i(\Phi_i) = +1. \label{eq:ir_regime_selector}$$

This is the classical regime: perturbations are tachyonic and amplified, corresponding to linear scaling, deterministic reasoning, and vivid qualia.

The Definition of

Based on the sign of the effective mass squared, the site-dependent regime selector is defined as:

$$\boxed{ s_i(\Phi_i) = \operatorname{sign}(-m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i)). } \label{eq:s_i_definition_HNN}$$

Equivalently:

$$\boxed{ s_i(\Phi_i) = \begin{cases} +1 & \text{if } m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) < 0 \quad \text{(classical regime, linear scaling)}, \\ -1 & \text{if } m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) > 0 \quad \text{(quantum regime, inverse scaling)}. \end{cases} } \label{eq:s_i_cases_HNN}$$

The crossover occurs when:

$$m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i) = 0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad 4V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} = \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i. \label{eq:crossover_condition_HNN}$$

This condition defines the quantum-classical interface on the conscious screen.

The site-dependent regime selector on the HNN is:

Proof. From $s_i = \operatorname{sign}(-m_{\rm eff}^2)$ and $m_{\rm eff}^2 = 4V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} - e^{-\Phi_i} R_i/(16\pi G)$:

The Reverse-Vision Process

The reverse-vision process is the most direct phenomenological manifestation of the holographic principle in the conscious brain. It is the top-down propagation of an internal UV spark that "paints" the conscious image without external sensory input. This process explains mental imagery, dreams, hallucinations, memory recall, and the stream of thought—all phenomena in which conscious experience is generated internally rather than triggered by external stimuli.

Definition of the Reverse-Vision Process

The reverse-vision process is the top-down holographic reconstruction of conscious experience from the 2D screen. Unlike forward perception—where external light enters the eye and propagates bottom-up through the visual pathway—reverse vision originates from an internal spark generated in higher-order frontal/parietal regions and propagates top-down to "paint" the conscious image.

The reverse-vision process is the top-down propagation of an internal UV spark that sources a -wave across the 2D conscious screen, causing each site to relax on-shell to the local Master equation, thereby painting the macroscopic conscious image.

The Four-Stage Mechanism

The reverse-vision process proceeds in four distinct stages, each governed by the equations of the HNN:

  1. Spark Generation: An internal UV spark is generated in higher-order frontal/parietal regions. This spark corresponds to a thought, intention, mental image, or memory retrieval.

  2. -Wave Propagation: The spark sources the discrete -evolution equation, generating a -wave that propagates top-down across the 2D screen.

  3. Local Relaxation: Each site relaxes on-shell to satisfy the local Master equation, adjusting to the value required by the spark.

  4. Image Painting: The resulting global pattern across the screen is the macroscopic conscious percept. The qualia field is the spatial average of this pattern.

Stage 1: Spark Generation

The internal spark is the source term in the -evolution equation:

$$T_i^{\rm internal\ spark}(t) = T_0 \, X_f(t) \, \delta(\mathbf{r}_i - \mathbf{r}_0), \label{eq:spark_source}$$

where:

  • is the amplitude of the spark (normalised),

  • is the time-dependent spark strength,

  • is the location of the spark source (frontal/parietal cortex),

  • is the Kronecker delta on the lattice.

The spark strength is a dimensionless quantity representing the normalized neural activation amplitude. It is generated by higher-order cognitive processes, including:

  • Volitional thought,

  • Memory retrieval,

  • Mental imagery,

  • Dream generation,

  • Creative insight,

  • Metacognitive reflection.

When the spark is self-referential ( ), it generates metacognitive awareness.

Stage 2: -Wave Propagation

The spark sources the discrete -evolution equation:

$$\Box_i \Phi_i = -\frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i + 2V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} + T_i^{\rm spark}(t). \label{eq:phi_wave_propagation}$$

The solution is a propagating -wave:

$$\Phi_i(t) = \Phi_i(0) + \Phi_{\rm wave} \cos(\mathbf{k} \cdot \mathbf{r}_i - \omega t) e^{-\gamma t}, \label{eq:phi_wave_solution}$$

where:

  • is the wave vector (direction of propagation),

  • is the angular frequency (determined by the spark),

  • is the damping coefficient (zero in ideal conditions),

  • is the position vector of site ,

  • $\Phi_{\rm wave}$ is the wave amplitude.

The dispersion relation is:

where is the system-specific causal velocity (bounded by axonal conduction velocity).

Stage 3: Local Relaxation

As the -wave passes through each site , the site relaxes on-shell to satisfy the local Master equation:

The relaxation is driven by the restoring term in the -evolution equation:

The regime selector determines whether the site relaxes in the quantum regime ( , inverse scaling) or the classical regime ( , linear scaling):

  • Quantum relaxation ( ): . Weak sparks produce amplified, creative, dream-like percepts.

  • Classical relaxation ( ): . Strong sparks produce vivid, deterministic, focused percepts.

The relaxation occurs on a timescale determined by the effective mass squared:

$$\tau_{\rm relax} \sim \frac{1}{|m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i)|}. \label{eq:relaxation_time}$$

Stage 4: Image Painting

After all sites have relaxed, the global pattern constitutes the macroscopic conscious percept. The local observables represent the subjective qualities of the percept:

  • Vividness of color,

  • Sharpness of sound,

  • Intensity of emotion,

  • Clarity of thought,

  • Strength of memory.

The global qualia field is the spatial average:

The intelligence fidelity is the same quantity:

The Complete Mathematical Description

The complete mathematical description of the reverse-vision process is the system of equations:

$$\boxed{ \begin{aligned} \text{Spark generation: } & T_i^{\rm spark}(t) = T_0 \, X_f(t) \, \delta(\mathbf{r}_i - \mathbf{r}_0), \\ \Phi\text{-wave propagation: } & \Box_i \Phi_i = -\frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i + 2V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} + T_i^{\rm spark}(t), \\ \text{Local relaxation: } & X_i = X_p'(\Phi_i,v_c)\left(\frac{X_f}{X_p'(X_f)}\right)^{s_i(\Phi_i)}, \\ \text{Image painting: } & \mathcal{Q} = \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^N X_i, \qquad I \equiv \mathcal{Q} \quad \text{(self-referential)}. \end{aligned} } \label{eq:reverse_vision_system}$$

Physical Interpretation

The reverse-vision process has several profound implications:

  1. Consciousness is holographic: The entire conscious experience is reconstructed from the 2D screen. There is no "central processor" or "Cartesian theatre"; consciousness is the global pattern of on the screen.

  2. Mental imagery is physical: Mental images, dreams, and hallucinations are not ethereal; they are -waves propagating on the cortical screen. They obey the same equations as perception.

  3. The stream of consciousness is deterministic: The -evolution equation is fully deterministic. The stream of consciousness is the on-shell evolution of the -field under internal sparks.

  4. Self-awareness is a loop: The self-referential loop is the physical basis of self-awareness. The "I" is the loop itself.

  5. No external input required: The reverse-vision process generates conscious experience without external sensory input. This explains dreams, daydreams, and mental imagery.

Summary of the HNN

The Holographic Neural Network is:

$$\boxed{ \begin{aligned} \text{Lattice: } & \mathcal{L} = \{\mathbf{r}_i \in \mathbb{R}^2 : i = 1, 2, \ldots, N\}, \quad N \sim 10^6, \\ \text{Lattice spacing: } & a \sim 0.5\ \text{mm}, \\ \text{Local variable: } & \Phi_i \in \mathbb{R}, \\ \text{Discrete curvature: } & R_i = \frac{1}{a^2} \sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}(i)} w_{ij} (\Phi_j - \Phi_i), \\ \text{Discrete d'Alembertian: } & \Box_i \Phi_i = \frac{1}{a^2} \sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}(i)} (\Phi_j - \Phi_i) - \frac{\partial^2 \Phi_i}{\partial t^2}, \\ \text{Potential: } & V(\Phi_i) = V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i}, \\ \text{Action: } & S_{\rm HNN} = \sum_i a^2 \sqrt{-g_i} \left[ \frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i - \frac{1}{2} e^{-\Phi_i} \sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}(i)} \frac{(\Phi_j - \Phi_i)^2}{a^2} - V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} \right], \\ \Phi\text{-evolution: } & \Box_i \Phi_i = -\frac{e^{-\Phi_i}}{16\pi G} R_i + 2V_0 e^{-2\Phi_i} + T_i^{\rm spark}(t), \\ \text{Regime selector: } & s_i(\Phi_i) = \operatorname{sign}(-m_{\rm eff}^2(\Phi_i)), \\ \text{Local Master: } & X_i = X_p'(\Phi_i,v_c)\left(\frac{X_f}{X_p'(X_f)}\right)^{s_i(\Phi_i)}, \\ \text{Global observable: } & I \equiv \mathcal{Q} = \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^N X_p'(\Phi_i,v_c)\left(\frac{X_f}{X_p'(X_f)}\right)^{s_i(\Phi_i)}. \end{aligned} }$$

The HNN is the complete physical substrate of consciousness, intelligence, and higher-order cognition. The next sections will derive the quantum computing, classical computing, and hybrid computing regimes, holographic error correction, the consciousness and intelligence models, and the higher-order abstractions.

"Big things have small beginnings."— Prometheus “‘
